quotes.sentence: My father said: "You're not worthy of my land and title. Tomorrow you're going to take the black, or we will have a hunt, and you'll be thrown from your saddle to die. Nothing would please me more."
quotes.originalSentence: On the morning of my eighteenth nameday, my father came to me. "You're almost a man now, he said, but you're not worthy of my land and title. Tomorrow you're going to take the black, forsake all claims to your inheritance and start north. If you do not," he said, "then we will have a hunt, and somewhere in these woods, your horse will stumble and you'll be thrown from your saddle to die. Or so I will tell your mother. Nothing would please me more."

quotes.originalSentence: Let's say Viserys Targaryen lands with 40,000 Dothraki screamers at his back. We hole up in our castles, a wise move, only a fool would meet the Dothraki in an open field. They leave us in our castles. They go from town to town, looting and burning, killing every man who can't hide behind a stone wall, stealing all our crops and livestock, enslaving all our women and children. How long do the people of the Seven Kingdoms stand behind their absentee King, their cowardly King hiding behind high walls? When do the people decide that Viserys Targaryen is the rightful monarch after all?!

Now we've got as many armies as there are men with gold in their purse. And everybody wants something different. Your father wants to own the world. Ned Stark wants to run away and bury his head in the snow.

quotes.originalSentence: At first, just saying her name, even in private, felt like I was breathing life back into her. I thought if I didn't talk about her, she'd just fade away for you. When I realized that wasn't going to happen, I refused to ask out of spite. I didn't want to give you the satisfaction of thinking I cared to ask. And eventually it became clear that my spite didn't mean anything to you; as far as I could tell, you actually enjoyed it!

I stole her robe. She was forced to return to the castle, naked and in tears.  If I close my eyes, I can still see her tits bouncing.

quotes.sentence: It's the family name that lives on. Not your honour, not your personal glory, family. I need you to become the man you were always meant to be. Not next year, not tomorrow, now.
quotes.originalSentence: No, it's not an opinion, it's a fact!  If another house can seize one of our own, and hold him captive with impunity, it means we're no longer a house to be feared! Your mother's dead, before long I'll be dead, and you and your brother and  your sister and all of her children. All of us dead, all of us rotting in the ground. It's the family name that lives on. It's all that lives on. Not your honour, not your personal glory, family. Do you understand? You're blessed with abilities that few men possess. You're blessed to belong to the most powerful family in the Kingdoms, and you're still blessed with youth. And what have you done with these blessings, hmm? You've served as a glorified bodyguard for two kings, one a madman, the other a drunk. The future of our family will be determined in these next few months. We could establish a dynasty that will last a thousand years...or we could collapse into nothing, as the Targaryens did. I need you to become the man you were always meant to be. Not next year, not tomorrow, now.

quotes.sentence: I sent 2,000 men to their graves today.

quotes.sentence: The bards will sing songs of their sacrifice.
quotes.originalSentence: The bards will sing songs of their sacrifice.

quotes.sentence: One victory does not make us conquerers. This war is far from over.
quotes.originalSentence: Aye, but the dead won't hear them. One victory does not make us conquerers. Did we free my father? Did we free my sisters from the Queen? Did we free the North from those who'd have us on our knees? This war is far from over.

quotes.sentence: Did you ever wonder why the Night's Watch take no wives?
quotes.originalSentence: Tell me, did you ever wonder why the men of the Night's Watch take no wives and father no children?

quotes.sentence: Love is the death of duty.
quotes.originalSentence: So they will not love. Love is the death of duty. lf the day should ever come when your lord father was forced to choose, between honour on the one hand and those he loves on the other, what would he do?

quotes.sentence: He would do whatever was right. No matter what.
quotes.originalSentence: He...He would do whatever was right. No matter what.

quotes.sentence: What is honour, compared to a woman's love? And what is duty, against a son in your arms?
quotes.originalSentence: Then Lord Stark is one man in 10,000. Most of us are not so strong. What is honour, compared to a woman's love? And what is duty, against the feel of a newborn son in your arms? Or a brother's smile?

quotes.sentence: Sam told you?
quotes.originalSentence: Sam told you?

quotes.sentence: In every man's life, there comes a day when he must choose.
quotes.originalSentence: We're all human. Oh, we all do our duty when there's no cost to it. Honour comes easy then. Yet, sooner or later in every man's life, there comes a day when it is not easy. A day when he must choose.

quotes.sentence: And this is my day?
quotes.originalSentence: And this is my day? ls that what you're saying?

quotes.sentence: Oh, it hurts, boy. Oh, yes. I know.
quotes.originalSentence: Oh, it hurts, boy. Oh, yes. I know.

quotes.sentence: I may be a bastard, but he is my father and Robb is my brother!
quotes.originalSentence: You do not know. No one knows. I may be a bastard, but he is my father and Robb is my brother!

quotes.sentence: The gods were cruel when they saw fit to test my vow. The ruin of my house. The death of my family. Even the little children!
quotes.originalSentence: The gods were cruel when they saw fit to test my vow. They waited until I was old. What could I do when the ravens brought the news from the South? The ruin of my house. The death of my family. I was helpless, blind, frail, but when I heard they had killed my brother's son, and his poor son, and the children. Even the little children!

quotes.sentence: My father was Maekar. My brother Aegon reigned after him. His son was Aerys, "The Mad King."
quotes.originalSentence: My father was Maekar, first of his name. My brother Aegon reigned after him, when I had refused the throne, and his son was Aerys whom they called "The Mad King."

quotes.sentence: You're Aemon Targaryen!
quotes.originalSentence: You're Aemon Targaryen!

quotes.sentence: I am a Maester of the Citadel. You must make that choice yourself.
quotes.originalSentence: I am a Maester of the Citadel, bound in service to Castle Black and the Night's Watch. I will not tell you to stay or go. You must make that choice yourself and live with it the rest of your days, as I have.

quotes.sentence: I come before you to confess my treason. Joffrey Baratheon is the one true heir to the Iron Throne.
quotes.originalSentence: I am Eddard Stark, Lord of Winterfell and Hand of the King. I come before you to confess my treason, in the sight of Gods and men.  I betrayed the faith of my King, and the trust of my friend, Robert. I swore to defend and protect his children, but before his blood was cold I plotted to murder his son, and seize the throne for myself. Let the High Septon and Baelor the Blessed bear witness to what I say...Joffrey Baratheon is the one true heir to the Iron Throne. By the grace of all the Gods, Lord of the Seven Kingdoms and Protector of the Realm.

quotes.sentence: So long as I am your King, treason shall never go unpunished! Ser Ilyn! Bring me his head!
quotes.originalSentence: My mother wishes me to let Lord Eddard join the Night's Watch. Stripped of all titles and powers, he would serve the Realm in permanent exile. And my Lady Sansa... has begged mercy for her father. But they have the soft hearts of women. So long as I am your King, treason shall never go unpunished! Ser Ilyn! Bring me his head!

quotes.sentence: You knew what I was buying, and you knew the price!
quotes.originalSentence: You knew what I was buying, and you knew the price!

quotes.sentence: This is not God's work. My child was innocent!
quotes.originalSentence: This is not God's work. My child was innocent!

quotes.sentence: I spoke for you. I saved you!
quotes.originalSentence: I spoke for you. I saved you!

quotes.sentence: Your life!
quotes.originalSentence: Your life!

quotes.sentence: They have my son.
quotes.originalSentence: They have my son.

quotes.sentence: The Stark boy appears to be less green than we'd hoped.
quotes.originalSentence: The Stark boy appears to be less green than we'd hoped.

quotes.sentence: Joffrey saw to that when he decided to remove Ned Stark's head. He's winning, in case you hadn't noticed.
quotes.originalSentence: There's your peace. Joffrey saw to that when he decided to remove Ned Stark's head. You'll have an easier time drinking out of that cup than you will bringing Robb Stark to the table now. He's winning, in case you hadn't noticed.

quotes.sentence: THEY HAVE MY SON! I always thought you were a stunted fool. Perhaps I was wrong.
quotes.originalSentence: THEY HAVE MY SON! Get out, all of you.  Not you. You were right about Eddard Stark; if he were alive, we could've used him to broker a peace with Winterfell and Riverrun... which would have given us more time to deal with Robert's brothers, but now? Madness, madness and stupidity! I always thought you were a stunted fool. Perhaps I was wrong.

quotes.sentence: Half wrong. Unless we want to be surrounded by three armies, we can't stay here.
quotes.originalSentence: Half wrong. I'm new to strategy, but unless we want to be surrounded by three armies, it appears we can't stay here.

quotes.sentence: No one will stay here. Ser Gregor will set the Riverlands on fire. You will go to King's Landing.
quotes.originalSentence: No one will stay here. Ser Gregor will head out with five hundred riders and set the Riverlands on fire from Gods' Eye to the Red Fork. The rest of us will regroup at Harrenhal. And you will go to King's Landing.

quotes.sentence: And do what?
quotes.originalSentence: And do what?

quotes.sentence: Rule. You will serve as Hand of the King. Bring that boy-king to heel.
quotes.originalSentence: Rule. You will serve as Hand of the King in my stead; you'll bring that boy-king to heel, and his mother too, if needs be. And if you get so much as a whiff of treason from any of the rest, Baelish, Varys, Pycelle...

quotes.sentence: Heads, spikes, walls. Why not anyone?! Why me?
quotes.originalSentence: Heads, spikes, walls. Why not my uncle? Why not anyone?! Why me?

quotes.sentence: You're my son. You will not take that whore to court.
quotes.originalSentence: You're my son. Oh, one more thing.  You will not take that whore to court.

quotes.sentence: Am I your brother, now and always?
quotes.originalSentence: Am I your brother, now and always?

quotes.sentence: Now and always, Theon.
quotes.originalSentence: Now and always, Theon.

quotes.sentence: Don't get up! More ravishing than ever, big sister! Excuse the interruption.
quotes.originalSentence: Don't get up! More ravishing than ever, big sister! War agrees with you!   Excuse the interruption. Carry on.

quotes.sentence: What are you doing here?
quotes.originalSentence: What are you doing here?

quotes.sentence: It's been a remarkable journey! I pissed off the edge of the Wall. I slept in a sky-cell.
quotes.originalSentence: It's been a... remarkable journey! I pissed off the edge of the Wall. I slept in a sky-cell. I fought with the hill tribes! So many adventures, so much to be thankful for!

quotes.sentence: What are you doing here? This is the Small Council.
quotes.originalSentence: What are you doing here? This is the Small Council.

quotes.sentence: I do believe the Hand of the King is welcome at all Small Council meetings.
quotes.originalSentence: Yes, well, I do believe the Hand of the King is welcome at all Small Council meetings.

quotes.sentence: Our father is Hand of the King!
quotes.originalSentence: Our father is Hand of the King!

quotes.sentence: Yes. But in his absence...
quotes.originalSentence: Yes. But in his absence...

quotes.sentence: Your father has named Lord Tyrion to serve as Hand in his stead.
quotes.originalSentence: Your father has named Lord Tyrion to serve as Hand in his stead while he fights—

quotes.sentence: OUT! All of you, out!
quotes.originalSentence: OUT! All of you, out!

quotes.sentence: I would like to know how you tricked Father into this!
quotes.originalSentence: I would like to know how you tricked Father into this!

quotes.sentence: If I were capable of tricking Father, I'd be emperor. You brought this on yourself.
quotes.originalSentence: If I were capable of tricking Father, I'd be emperor of the world by now. You brought this on yourself.

quotes.sentence: I've done nothing.
quotes.originalSentence: I've done nothing.

quotes.sentence: You did nothing... when your son called for Ned Stark's head. Now the North has risen against us.
quotes.originalSentence: Quite right, you did nothing... when your son called for Ned Stark's head. Now the entire North has risen up against us.

quotes.sentence: I tried to stop it.
quotes.originalSentence: I tried to stop it.

quotes.sentence: You failed. That bit of theatre will haunt our family for a generation.
quotes.originalSentence: Did you? You failed. That bit of theatre will haunt our family for a generation.

quotes.sentence: Robb Stark is a child.
quotes.originalSentence: Robb Stark is a child.

quotes.sentence: Who's won every battle he's fought! Do you understand we're losing the war?
quotes.originalSentence: Who's won every battle he's fought! Do you understand we're losing the war?

quotes.sentence: What do you know about warfare?
quotes.originalSentence: What do you know about warfare?

quotes.sentence: I know people, and I know that our enemies hate each other almost as much as they hate us.
quotes.originalSentence: Nothing. But I know people, and I know that our enemies hate each other almost as much as they hate us.

quotes.sentence: Joffrey is King.
quotes.originalSentence: Joffrey is King.

quotes.sentence: You are here to advise him.
quotes.originalSentence: You are here to advise him.

quotes.sentence: If the King listens, he might just get his uncle Jaime back.
quotes.originalSentence: Only here to advise him. And if the King listens to what I say, the King might just get his uncle Jaime back.

quotes.sentence: You love your children. It's your one redeeming quality. That and your cheekbones.
quotes.originalSentence: You love your children. It’s your one redeeming quality. That and your cheekbones. The Starks love their children as well, and we have two of them.

quotes.sentence: Arya, that little animal, she disappeared.
quotes.originalSentence: Arya, that little animal, she disappeared.

quotes.sentence: Must be hard for you, to be the disappointing child.
quotes.originalSentence: Disappeared? What, in a puff of smoke?! We had three Starks to trade. You chopped one’s head off, and let another escape. Father would be furious. Must be hard for you, to be the disappointing child.

quotes.sentence: I don't dream.
quotes.originalSentence: I don't dream.

quotes.sentence: They say it's an omen. Robb will win a great victory.
quotes.originalSentence: I don't. I heard some of the men talking about the comet.  They say it's an omen. They say it means Robb will win a great victory in the South.

quotes.sentence: The dragons are all dead.
quotes.originalSentence: The dragons are all dead. They've been dead for centuries.

quotes.sentence: He wasn't my beloved brother. I didn't love him.
quotes.originalSentence: He wasn't my beloved brother.  I didn't love him, he didn't love me.

quotes.sentence: A harmless courtesy, Your Grace.
quotes.originalSentence: A harmless courtesy, Your Grace.

quotes.sentence: A lie. Take it out.
quotes.originalSentence: A lie.  Take it out.

quotes.sentence: Jaime Lannister, the Kingslayer. Call him what he is.
quotes.originalSentence: Jaime Lannister, the Kingslayer. Call him what he is.

quotes.sentence: Make it Ser Jaime Lannister, the Kingslayer.
quotes.originalSentence: Make it Ser Jaime Lannister, the Kingslayer. Whatever else he is, the man's still a knight.

quotes.sentence: The time has come to choose. Let no man claim ignorance as an excuse.
quotes.originalSentence: When Eddard Stark learned the truth, he told only me. I'll not make the same mistake. Send copies of that letter to every corner of the Realm, from the Arbor to the Wall. The time has come to choose. Let no man claim ignorance as an excuse.

quotes.sentence: Your Grace, the Lannisters are the true enemy.
quotes.originalSentence: Your Grace, the Lannisters are the true enemy. If, for the time being, you could make peace with your brother—

quotes.sentence: I'll not make peace with Renly while he calls himself King.
quotes.originalSentence: I'll not make peace with Renly while he calls himself King.

quotes.sentence: Many have already declared for him; Mace Tyrell, Randyll Tarly...
quotes.originalSentence: Well, many have already declared for him; Mace Tyrell, Randyll Tarly...

quotes.sentence: The Lord of Light stands behind him.
quotes.originalSentence: Stannis does not need to beg this lord or that lord for support. The Lord of Light stands behind him.

quotes.sentence: How many ships has the Lord of Light got in his fleet?
quotes.originalSentence: And how many ships has the Lord of Light got in his fleet?

quotes.sentence: He has no need for ships.
quotes.originalSentence: He has no need for ships.

quotes.sentence: We do if we're going to war. Join forces with Robb Stark.
quotes.originalSentence: I'm sure he doesn't, but we do if we're going to war.  If not Renly, Your Grace, join forces with Robb Stark.

quotes.sentence: Joffrey, Renly, Robb Stark; they're all thieves. They'll bend the knee or I'll destroy them.
quotes.originalSentence: Who would steal the northern half of my kingdom. I've always served thieves according to their desserts–as you well know, Ser Davos. Joffrey, Renly, Robb Stark; they're all thieves. They'll bend the knee or I'll destroy them.

quotes.sentence: Don't.
quotes.originalSentence: Don't.

quotes.sentence: The night is dark and full of terrors.
quotes.originalSentence: The night is dark and full of terrors, old man.  But the fires burn them all away.

quotes.sentence: Have you grown fond of me, Stark? I've never seen you with a girl.
quotes.originalSentence: The King in the North. I keep expecting you to leave me in one castle or another for safekeeping, but you drag me along from camp to camp. Have you grown fond of me, Stark? Is that it? I've never seen you with a girl.

quotes.sentence: Refuse, and your house'll be destroyed, root and stem.
quotes.originalSentence: If I left you with one of my bannermen, your father would know within a fortnight, and my bannermen would receive a raven with a message: "Release my son and you'll be rich beyond your dreams. Refuse, and your house'll be destroyed, root and stem."

quotes.sentence: You don't trust the loyalty of the men following you?
quotes.originalSentence: You don't trust the loyalty of the men following you into battle?

quotes.sentence: I trust them with my life, just not with yours.
quotes.originalSentence: Oh, I trust them with my life, just not with yours.

quotes.sentence: Smart, boy. Don't like being called boy? Insulted?
quotes.originalSentence: Smart, boy. What's wrong? Don't like being called boy? Insulted?

quotes.sentence: You insult yourself, Kingslayer. Perhaps you'll be killed by a boy.
quotes.originalSentence: You insult yourself, Kingslayer. You've been defeated by a boy. You're held captive by a boy. Perhaps you'll be killed by a boy.

quotes.sentence: King Joffrey is neither a true King, nor a true Baratheon. He's your bastard son.
quotes.originalSentence: Stannis Baratheon sent ravens to all the High Lords of Westeros. King Joffrey Baratheon is neither a true King, nor a true Baratheon. He's your bastard son.

quotes.sentence: If that's true, Stannis is the rightful King. How convenient for him.
quotes.originalSentence: Well, if that's true, Stannis is the rightful King. How convenient for him.

quotes.sentence: My father learned the truth. That's why you had him executed.
quotes.originalSentence: My father learned the truth. That's why you had him executed.

quotes.sentence: I was your prisoner when Ned Stark lost his head.
quotes.originalSentence: I was your prisoner when Ned Stark lost his head.

quotes.sentence: You pushed my brother from a window because he saw you with the Queen.
quotes.originalSentence: Your son killed him so the world wouldn't learn who fathered him and you... you pushed my brother from a window because he saw you with the Queen.

quotes.sentence: You have proof? Or, do you want to trade gossip?
quotes.originalSentence: You have proof? Or, do you want to trade gossip like a couple of fishwives?

quotes.sentence: I'm sending one of your cousins to King's Landing with my peace terms.
quotes.originalSentence: I'm sending one of your cousins down to King's Landing with my peace terms.

quotes.sentence: You think my father's going to negotiate with you? You don't know him very well.
quotes.originalSentence: You think my father's going to negotiate with you? You don't know him very well.

quotes.sentence: No, but he's starting to know me.
quotes.originalSentence: No, but he's starting to know me.

quotes.sentence: It's better than three defeats.
quotes.originalSentence: It's better than three defeats.

quotes.sentence: First, your family must release my sisters. Second, my father's bones must be returned.
quotes.originalSentence: I offer your cousins peace, if they meet my terms. First, your family must release my sisters. Second, my father's bones must be returned to us, so that he may rest beside his brother and sister in the crypts beneath Winterfell. And the remains of all those who died in his service must be returned also, that their families can honour them with proper funerals.

quotes.sentence: We are a free and independent kingdom.
quotes.originalSentence: Third, Joffrey and the Queen Regent must renounce all claim to dominion of the North. From this time to the end of time, we are a free and independent kingdom.

quotes.sentence: If he disregards this command, he shall suffer the same fate as my father.
quotes.originalSentence: Neither Joffrey nor any of his men shall ever set foot in our lands again. If he disregards this command, he shall suffer the same fate as my father...only I won't need a servant to do my beheading for me!

quotes.sentence: These are my terms! If not, I will litter the south with Lannister dead.
quotes.originalSentence: These are my terms! If the Queen Regent and her son meet them, I'll give them peace. If not, I will litter the south with Lannister dead.

quotes.sentence: Is he?
quotes.originalSentence: Is he?

quotes.sentence: I don't like threats.
quotes.originalSentence: I don't like threats.

quotes.sentence: Who threatened you?
quotes.originalSentence: Who threatened you?

quotes.sentence: I'm not Ned Stark, I understand the way this game is played.
quotes.originalSentence: I'm not Ned Stark, I understand the way this game is played.

quotes.sentence: Ned Stark was a man of honour.
quotes.originalSentence: Ned Stark was a man of honour.

quotes.sentence: Threaten me again and I'll have you thrown into the sea!
quotes.originalSentence: And I am not. Threaten me again and I'll have you thrown into the sea!

quotes.sentence: The storms come and go, the big fish eat the little fish, and I keep on paddling.
quotes.originalSentence: You might be disappointed in the results. The storms come and go, the big fish eat the little fish, and I keep on paddling.

quotes.sentence: Then I wouldn't get to insult anyone.
quotes.originalSentence: Then I wouldn't get to insult anyone.

quotes.sentence: Who asked questions before?
quotes.originalSentence: No good's ever come? Who asked questions before?

quotes.sentence: Who asked questions?
quotes.originalSentence: Who asked questions?

quotes.sentence: Lord Stark?
quotes.originalSentence: Lord Stark?

quotes.sentence: What did they ask about?
quotes.originalSentence: What did they ask about?

quotes.sentence: Who's your mum?
quotes.originalSentence: Who's your mum?

quotes.sentence: And who was your father?
quotes.originalSentence: And who was your father?

quotes.sentence: I'm not a girl!
quotes.originalSentence: I'm not a girl!

quotes.sentence: Night's Watch doesn't take girls. Everyone knows that.
quotes.originalSentence: Stupider. Night's Watch doesn't take girls. everyone knows that.

quotes.sentence: I am not!
quotes.originalSentence: I am not!

quotes.sentence: Lommy and Hot Pie can't know. No one can know.
quotes.originalSentence: I don't need to take a piss... Lommy and Hot Pie can't know. No one can know.

quotes.sentence: My name's not Arry. It's Arya. Of House Stark.
quotes.originalSentence: My name's not Arry. It's Arya. Of House Stark. Yoren is taking me home to Winterfell.

quotes.sentence: He was never a traitor! Joffrey is a liar!
quotes.originalSentence: He was never a traitor! Joffrey is a liar!

quotes.sentence: My mother was a lady, and my sister—
quotes.originalSentence: No–I mean, yes. My mother was a lady, and my sister—

quotes.sentence: Do not call me, m'lady.
quotes.originalSentence: Do not call me, m'lady.

quotes.sentence: The Queen?
quotes.originalSentence: The Queen?

quotes.sentence: I promise you the gold. I promise you the glory. I cannot promise you the Queen.
quotes.originalSentence: Ah, he's young yet. I promise you the gold. I promise you the glory. I cannot promise you the Queen.

quotes.sentence: He is the one, true king.
quotes.originalSentence: He is the one, true king.

quotes.sentence: Get me to the gates of King's Landing, and I will.
quotes.originalSentence: Get me to the gates of King's Landing, and I will.

quotes.sentence: I heard there was some trouble in Littlefinger's brothel...
quotes.originalSentence: I heard there was some trouble in Littlefinger's brothel the other night.

quotes.sentence: I hadn't realized peace depended on killing babies.
quotes.originalSentence: Yes, of course. The City Watch must keep the peace. Only, I hadn't realized peace depended on killing babies.

quotes.sentence: Quite right, especially the Queen's orders.
quotes.originalSentence: Quite right, especially the Queen's orders.

quotes.sentence: Who else would want to murder King Robert's bastards?
quotes.originalSentence: No, but who else would want to murder King Robert's bastards? She's always been a jealous woman.

quotes.sentence: You've heard the awful rumors about my brother and sister.
quotes.originalSentence: You've heard the awful rumors about my brother and sister.

quotes.sentence: People who believe that filth consider Robert's bastards to be better claimants...
quotes.originalSentence: Well, that's good of you, but you have heard them. I suppose people who do believe that filth consider Robert's bastards to be better claimants to the Throne than Cersei's children.

quotes.sentence: When your men slaughtered Ned Stark's men, did you give the order?
quotes.originalSentence: I appreciate your loyalty. Tell me, when your men slaughtered Ned Stark's men in the throne room, did you give the order?

quotes.sentence: The fool! He had no idea you were already bought.
quotes.originalSentence: The fool! He had no idea you were already bought.

quotes.sentence: I'm not questioning your honour, I'm denying its existence.
quotes.originalSentence: I'm not questioning your honour, Lord Janos; I'm denying its existence.

quotes.sentence: "Dwarf"? You should've stopped at "Imp"!
quotes.originalSentence: "Dwarf"? You should've stopped at "Imp"!  And, yes, you will stand here and take it from me, unless you'd like to take it from my friend here!

quotes.sentence: I intend to serve as Hand of the King... I just wouldn't feel safe with you lurking about.
quotes.originalSentence: I intend to serve as Hand of the King until my father returns from the war. And seeing as you betrayed the last Hand of the King, well, I just wouldn't feel safe with you lurking about.

quotes.sentence: You're a fool to believe she is your friend.
quotes.originalSentence: The Queen Regent. And you're a fool to believe she is your friend.

quotes.sentence: I hope you enjoy the Wall. I found it surprisingly beautiful. In a brutal, horribly uncomfortable sort of way.
quotes.originalSentence: No, we shan't.  There's a ship leaving for Eastwatch-by-the-Sea tonight. From there, I'm afraid, it's rather a long walk to Castle Black. I hope you enjoy the Wall. I found it surprisingly beautiful. In a brutal, horribly uncomfortable sort of way.

quotes.sentence: His name is Bronn, and he is the new Commander of the City Watch.
quotes.originalSentence: His name is Bronn, and he is the new Commander of the City Watch.

quotes.sentence: If I told you to murder an infant girl, would you do it?
quotes.originalSentence: To the new Commander.  If I told you to murder an infant girl, say, still at her mother's breast, would you do it, without question?

quotes.sentence: Lord Janos Slynt was Commander of the City Watch. You had no right to exile him!
quotes.originalSentence: Lord Janos Slynt was Commander of the City Watch. You had no right to exile him!

quotes.sentence: I have every right. I am the King's Hand.
quotes.originalSentence: I have every right. I am the King's Hand.

quotes.sentence: You're serving as the King's Hand until Father gets here. I am the Queen Regent.
quotes.originalSentence: You're serving as the King's Hand until Father gets here. I am the Queen Regent.

quotes.sentence: Listen to me, Queen Regent. You're losing the people.
quotes.originalSentence: Listen to me, Queen Regent. You're losing the people. Do you hear me?

quotes.sentence: The people? You think I care?
quotes.originalSentence: The people? You think I care?

quotes.sentence: You might find it difficult to rule over millions who want you dead... 'The Queen slaughters babies!'
quotes.originalSentence: You might find it difficult to rule over millions who want you dead. Half this city will starve when winter comes, the other half will plot to overthrow you...and your gold-plated thugs just gave them their rallying cry. "The Queen slaughters babies!" You don't even have the decency to deny it! It wasn't you who gave the order, was it? Joffrey didn't even tell you? Did he tell you? I imagine that would be even worse.

quotes.sentence: This is what ruling is, lying on a bed of weeds, ripping them out by the root, one by one, before they strangle you in your sleep.
quotes.originalSentence: He did what needed to be done. You want to be Hand of the King? You want to rule? This is what ruling is, lying on a bed of weeds, ripping them out by the root, one by one, before they strangle you in your sleep.

quotes.sentence: I'm no king, but I think there's more to ruling than that.
quotes.originalSentence: I'm no king, but I think there's more to ruling than that.

quotes.sentence: I DON'T CARE WHAT YOU THINK! It's all fallen on me.
quotes.originalSentence: I DON'T CARE WHAT YOU THINK! You've never taken it seriously. You haven't, Jaime hasn't. It's all fallen on me.

quotes.sentence: As has Jaime repeatedly, according to Stannis Baratheon.
quotes.originalSentence: As has Jaime repeatedly, according to Stannis Baratheon.

quotes.sentence: Do you remember, back when you ripped my mother open on your way out of her and she bled to death?
quotes.originalSentence: You're funny. You've always been funny... but none of your jokes will ever match the first one, will they? Do you remember, back when you ripped my mother open on your way out of her and she bled to death?

quotes.sentence: She was my mother, too.
quotes.originalSentence: She was my mother, too.

quotes.sentence: No bigger joke in the world than that.
quotes.originalSentence: Mother gone, for the sake of you. There's no bigger joke in the world than that.

quotes.sentence: I'm walking, running, but I'm not me.
quotes.originalSentence: Every night it's the same: I'm walking, running, but I'm not me. I'm running through the Godswood, sniffing the dirt, tasting blood in my mouth when I've made a fresh kill, howling. Old Nan used to tell me stories about magical people who could live inside stags, birds, wolves.

quotes.sentence: So she was lying? They don't exist?
quotes.originalSentence: So she was lying? They don't exist?

quotes.sentence: My dreams are different. Mine are true.
quotes.originalSentence: No. My dreams are different. Mine are true. I dreamt of my father dying and Rickon had the same dream.

quotes.sentence: "We Do Not Sow".
quotes.originalSentence: "We Do Not Sow".

quotes.sentence: You gave me away like I was some dog you didn't want anymore.
quotes.originalSentence: You act as if I volunteered to go. You gave me away if you remember. The day you bent the knee to Robert Baratheon. After he crushed you. Did you take what was yours then?  You gave me away! Your boy! Your last boy! You gave me away like I was some dog you didn't want anymore, and now you curse me because I've come home!!

quotes.sentence: I can trust you, Pycelle. Can I not?
quotes.originalSentence: I can trust you, Pycelle. Can I not?

quotes.sentence: These are perilous times. The Crown must forge new alliances, sealed in matrimony.
quotes.originalSentence: These are perilous times. And the Crown must forge new alliances. And these alliances must often be sealed in matrimony.

quotes.sentence: The Queen mustn't know.
quotes.originalSentence: I'm trusting the council with these plans, but the Queen mustn't know. We can't have her meddling in affairs that could determine the future of the Realm. There's too much at stake.

quotes.sentence: I'm brokering an alliance with House Martell.
quotes.originalSentence: I'm brokering an alliance with House Martell of Dorne. Princess Myrcella will wed their youngest son when she comes of age, ensuring their loyalty and their army, should we need it.

quotes.sentence: But remember, the Queen mustn't know.
quotes.originalSentence: But remember, the Queen mustn't know.

quotes.sentence: Ooh, "The Queen mustn't know." I love conversations that begin this way.
quotes.originalSentence: Ooh, "The Queen mustn't know." I love conversations that begin this way.

quotes.sentence: I plan to marry Princess Myrcella off to Theon Greyjoy.
quotes.originalSentence: I plan to marry Princess Myrcella off to Theon Greyjoy.

quotes.sentence: Theon Greyjoy? Forgive me, my lord, but how?
quotes.originalSentence: Theon Greyjoy? Forgive me, my lord, but how? He grew up in Winterfell. He fights for Robb Stark.

quotes.sentence: But remember, you must tell no one.
quotes.originalSentence: Precisely.  Theon's father loathes the Starks and will convince the boy to come to our side. Greyjoy can destroy the Northern army from within and we can have his father's ships. But remember, you must tell no one.

quotes.sentence: Tell no one what?
quotes.originalSentence: Tell no one what?

quotes.sentence: I plan to wed Princess Myrcella to Robin Arryn of the Vale.
quotes.originalSentence: I plan to wed Princess Myrcella to Robin Arryn of the Vale. Lysa is not fond of me. But perhaps the promise of a royal match will convince her to let bygones be bygones.

quotes.sentence: She imprisoned you. She tried to execute you, and you offer her son a princess?
quotes.originalSentence: She imprisoned you. She tried to execute you, and you offer her son a princess?

quotes.sentence: For men in our position, holding grudges can be an encumbrance.
quotes.originalSentence: For men in our position, holding grudges can be an encumbrance, don't you think?

quotes.sentence: And I suppose you want me to broker this agreement?
quotes.originalSentence: And I suppose you want me to broker this agreement?

quotes.sentence: Who better?
quotes.originalSentence: Who better?

quotes.sentence: Yes, I could sing this song to Lysa, if I cared to. What's in it for me?
quotes.originalSentence: Yes, I could sing this song to Lysa, if I cared to. What's in it for me?

quotes.sentence: The gratitude of the people of Westeros... and Harrenhal.
quotes.originalSentence: The gratitude of the people of Westeros for helping to end this war, the adoration of the King for bringing the Vale back into the fold... and Harrenhal.

quotes.sentence: Harrenhal is cursed.
quotes.originalSentence: Harrenhal is cursed.

quotes.sentence: Never took you for a superstitious man. I plan to make you Lord of the Riverlands.
quotes.originalSentence: Never took you for a superstitious man. By all means, tear it down and rebuild. You'll be able to afford it. I plan to make you Lord of the Riverlands.

quotes.sentence: With a single stroke, you'd make me one of the greatest lords in the Realm.
quotes.originalSentence: With a single stroke, you'd make me one of the greatest lords in the Realm.

quotes.sentence: You served my family well in the matter of the succession.
quotes.originalSentence: You served my family well in the matter of the succession.

quotes.sentence: So did Janos Slynt, and he was given Harrenhal, too.
quotes.originalSentence: So did Janos Slynt, and he was given Harrenhal, too. Until you snatched it away.

quotes.sentence: I need you to deliver Lysa Arryn. It's settled, then? Good. Oh, and remember—
quotes.originalSentence: I need you to deliver Lysa Arryn. I didn't need Janos Slynt. It's settled, then?  Good. Oh, and remember—

quotes.sentence: The Queen mustn't know.
quotes.originalSentence: The Queen mustn't know.

quotes.sentence: You monster! Myrcella is my only daughter! Do you really think I will let you sell her like a common whore?!
quotes.originalSentence: You monster! Myrcella is my only daughter! Do you really think I will let you sell her like a common whore?!

quotes.sentence: Myrcella is a princess. Some would say she was born for this.
quotes.originalSentence: Myrcella is a princess. Some would say she was born for this.

quotes.sentence: I will not let you ship her off to Dorne as I was shipped off to Robert Baratheon!
quotes.originalSentence: I will not let you ship her off to Dorne as I was shipped off to Robert Baratheon!

quotes.sentence: Dorne is the safest place for her.
quotes.originalSentence: Dorne is the safest place for her.

quotes.sentence: Are you mad? The Martells loathe us.
quotes.originalSentence: Are you mad? The Martells loathe us.

quotes.sentence: That's why we need to seduce them. We're going to need their support.
quotes.originalSentence: That's why we need to seduce them. We're going to need their support in the war your son started.

quotes.sentence: She'll be a hostage.
quotes.originalSentence: She'll be a hostage.

quotes.sentence: A guest.
quotes.originalSentence: A guest.

quotes.sentence: You won't get away with this. Ned Stark had a piece of paper, too.
quotes.originalSentence: You won't get away with this. You think the piece of paper father gave you keeps you safe? Ned Stark had a piece of paper, too.

quotes.sentence: It's done, Cersei.
quotes.originalSentence: It's done, Cersei.

quotes.sentence: You cannot stop it!
quotes.originalSentence: You cannot stop it!

quotes.sentence: They will mount her pretty little head on a spike right beside yours.
quotes.originalSentence: Just how safe do you think Myrcella is if the city falls? Do you want to see her raped and butchered like the Targaryen children? Make no mistake, they will mount her pretty little head on a spike right beside yours.

quotes.sentence: Get out! Get out!
quotes.originalSentence: Get out!  Get out!

quotes.sentence: Power is a curious thing, my lord. Are you fond of riddles?
quotes.originalSentence: Power is a curious thing, my lord. Are you fond of riddles?

quotes.sentence: Why, am I about to hear one?
quotes.originalSentence: Why, am I about to hear one?

quotes.sentence: Three great men sit in a room; a king, a priest and a rich man... Who lives, who dies?
quotes.originalSentence: Three great men sit in a room; a king, a priest and a rich man. Between them stands a common sellsword. Each great man bids the sellsword kill the other two. Who lives, who dies?

quotes.sentence: Depends on the sellsword.
quotes.originalSentence: Depends on the sellsword.

quotes.sentence: Does it? He has neither crown, nor gold, nor the favour of the gods.
quotes.originalSentence: Does it? He has neither crown, nor gold, nor the favour of the gods.

quotes.sentence: He has a sword, the power of life and death.
quotes.originalSentence: He has a sword, the power of life and death.

quotes.sentence: But if it's swordsmen who rule, why do we pretend kings hold all the power? When Ned Stark lost his head, who was truly responsible?
quotes.originalSentence: But if it's swordsmen who rule, why do we pretend kings hold all the power? When Ned Stark lost his head, who was truly responsible? Joffrey? The executioner? Or something else?

quotes.sentence: I've decided I don't like riddles.
quotes.originalSentence: I've decided I don't like riddles.

quotes.sentence: We're not executing prisoners, Lord Bolton.
quotes.originalSentence: We're not executing prisoners, Lord Bolton.

quotes.sentence: I doubt it.
quotes.originalSentence: I doubt it.

quotes.sentence: My father outlawed flaying in the North.
quotes.originalSentence: My father outlawed flaying in the North.

quotes.sentence: We're not torturing them!
quotes.originalSentence: We're not torturing them!

quotes.sentence: The Lannisters hold prisoners of their own. I won't give them an excuse to abuse my sisters.
quotes.originalSentence: The Lannisters hold prisoners of their own. I won't give them an excuse to abuse my sisters.

quotes.sentence: What is the meaning of this? What kind of knight beats a helpless girl?
quotes.originalSentence: What is the meaning of this? What kind of knight beats a helpless girl?

quotes.sentence: Someone get the girl something to cover herself with. She's to be your Queen. Have you no regard for her honour?
quotes.originalSentence: Someone get the girl something to cover herself with.  She's to be your Queen. Have you no regard for her honour?

quotes.sentence: I'm punishing her.
quotes.originalSentence: I'm punishing her.

quotes.sentence: For what crimes? She did not fight her brother's battle, you halfwit!
quotes.originalSentence: For what crimes? She did not fight her brother's battle, you halfwit!

quotes.sentence: You can't talk to me like that! The king can do as he likes!
quotes.originalSentence: You can't talk to me like that! The king can do as he likes!

quotes.sentence: The Mad King did as he liked. Has your Uncle Jaime ever told you what happened to him?
quotes.originalSentence: The Mad King did as he liked. Has your Uncle Jaime ever told you what happened to him?

quotes.sentence: Bronn, the next time Ser Meryn speaks, kill him. That was a threat. See the difference?
quotes.originalSentence: I'm not threatening the King, ser. I'm educating my nephew. Bronn, the next time Ser Meryn speaks, kill him.  That was a threat. See the difference?

quotes.sentence: I apologize for my nephew's behaviour. Tell me the truth. Do you want an end to this engagement?
quotes.originalSentence: I apologize for my nephew's behaviour. Tell me the truth. Do you want an end to this engagement?

quotes.sentence: I am loyal to King Joffrey, my one true love.
quotes.originalSentence: I am loyal to King Joffrey, my one true love.

quotes.sentence: Lady Stark... You may survive us yet.
quotes.originalSentence: Lady Stark... You may survive us yet.

quotes.sentence: Lady Stark, I had not thought to find you in the Stormlands.
quotes.originalSentence: Lady Stark, I had not thought to find you in the Stormlands.

quotes.sentence: I had not thought to be here, Lord Stannis.
quotes.originalSentence: I had not thought to be here, Lord Stannis.

quotes.sentence: Can that truly be you?
quotes.originalSentence: Can that truly be you?

quotes.sentence: Who else might it be?
quotes.originalSentence: Who else might it be?

quotes.sentence: When I saw your standard, I couldn't be sure. Whose banner is that?
quotes.originalSentence: When I saw your standard, I couldn't be sure. Whose banner is that?

quotes.sentence: My own.
quotes.originalSentence: My own.

quotes.sentence: I suppose if we use the same one, the battle will be terribly confusing. Why is your stag on fire?
quotes.originalSentence: I suppose if we use the same one, the battle will be terribly confusing. Why is your stag on fire?

quotes.sentence: The king has taken for his sigil the fiery heart of the Lord of Light.
quotes.originalSentence: The king has taken for his sigil the fiery heart of the Lord of Light.

quotes.sentence: Ah, you must be this fire priestess we hear so much about. Ah brother, now I understand why you found religion in your old age!
quotes.originalSentence: Ah, you must be this fire priestess we hear so much about. Ah brother, now I understand why you found religion in your old age!

quotes.sentence: Watch yourself, Renly!
quotes.originalSentence: Watch yourself, Renly!

quotes.sentence: No, no, I'm relieved. I never really believed you were a fanatic; charmless, rigid, a bore, yes, but not a godly man.
quotes.originalSentence: No, no, I'm relieved. I never really believed you were a fanatic; charmless, rigid, a bore, yes, but not a godly man.

quotes.sentence: You should kneel before your brother. He is the Lord's Chosen, born amidst salt and smoke.
quotes.originalSentence: You should kneel before your brother. He is the Lord's Chosen, born amidst salt and smoke.

quotes.sentence: "Born amidst salt and smoke?" Is he a ham?
quotes.originalSentence: "Born amidst salt and smoke?" Is he a ham?

quotes.sentence: That's twice I've warned you.
quotes.originalSentence: That's twice I've warned you.

quotes.sentence: Listen to yourselves! If you were sons of mine, I would knock your heads together and lock you in a bedchamber together until you remember you're brothers.
quotes.originalSentence: Listen to yourselves! If you were sons of mine, I would knock your heads together and lock you in a bedchamber together until you remember you're brothers.

quotes.sentence: It is strange to find you beside my brother, Lady Stark. Your husband was a supporter of my claim; Lord Eddard's integrity cost him his head. And you sit beside this pretender and chastise me?
quotes.originalSentence: It is strange to find you beside my brother, Lady Stark. Your husband was a supporter of my claim; Lord Eddard's integrity cost him his head. And you sit beside this pretender and chastise me?

quotes.sentence: We share a common enemy!
quotes.originalSentence: We share a common enemy!

quotes.sentence: The Iron Throne is mine by right. All those who deny that are my foes.
quotes.originalSentence: The Iron Throne is mine by right. All those who deny that are my foes.

quotes.sentence: The whole realm denies it... No one wants you for their king. A man without friends is a man without power.
quotes.originalSentence: The whole realm denies it, from Dorne to the Wall. Old men deny it with their death rattles and unborn children deny it in their mothers' wombs. No one wants you for their king. You never wanted any friends, brother, but a man without friends is a man without power.

quotes.sentence: For the sake of the mother who bore us, I will give you one night to reconsider. Otherwise I shall destroy you.
quotes.originalSentence: For the sake of the mother who bore us, I will give you this one night to reconsider. Strike your banners, come to me before dawn and I will grant you your old seat on the Council. I'll even name you my heir, until a son is born to me. Otherwise I shall destroy you.

quotes.sentence: Look across those fields, brother. Can you see all those banners?
quotes.originalSentence: Look across those fields, brother. Can you see all those banners?

quotes.sentence: You think a few bolts of cloth will make you king?
quotes.originalSentence: You think a few bolts of cloth will make you king?

quotes.sentence: No. The men holding those bolts of cloth will make me king!
quotes.originalSentence: No. The men holding those bolts of cloth will make me king!

quotes.sentence: We shall see, Renly. Come the dawn, we shall see.
quotes.originalSentence: We shall see, Renly. Come the dawn, we shall see.

quotes.sentence: Look to your sins, Lord Renly. The night is dark and full of terrors.
quotes.originalSentence: Look to your sins, Lord Renly. The night is dark and full of terrors.

quotes.sentence: Would you believe? I loved him once.
quotes.originalSentence: Would you believe? I loved him once.

quotes.sentence: An anointed Knight, yes. Tell me, did Cersei have you knighted before or after she took you into her bed?
quotes.originalSentence: An anointed Knight, yes. Tell me, did Cersei have you knighted before or after she took you into her bed?  What? Nothing to say? No more warnings for me, Ser?

quotes.sentence: Have you ever given any thought to what King Joffrey will have to say when he finds out you have been bedding his mother?
quotes.originalSentence: Have you ever given any thought to what King Joffrey will have to say when he finds out you have been bedding his mother?

quotes.sentence: Did she take you against your will? Can you not defend yourself, Knight?
quotes.originalSentence: Did she take you against your will? Can you not defend yourself, Knight?

quotes.sentence: Did he tell you to fuck her, too?
quotes.originalSentence: Did he tell you to fuck her, too?

quotes.sentence: Hated every moment of it, is that what you'll have me believe? A high place in court, a knighthood, my sister's legs spreading open for you at night! Oh, yes, it must have been terrible!
quotes.originalSentence: Hated every moment of it, is that what you'll have me believe? A high place in court, a knighthood, my sister's legs spreading open for you at night! Oh, yes, it must have been terrible! Wait here, His Grace will want to hear this.

quotes.sentence: Save it for Joffrey; he loves a good grovel.
quotes.originalSentence: Save it for Joffrey; he loves a good grovel.

quotes.sentence: No, I think not.
quotes.originalSentence: No, I think not.

quotes.sentence: My father told you to obey my sister. Stay close to her side. Keep her trust. I want to know what Cersei is doing, and you will tell me.
quotes.originalSentence: You heard me. My father told you to obey my sister. Obey her. Stay close to her side. Keep her trust. Pleasure her whenever she requires. No one ever need know as long as you keep faith with me. I want to know what Cersei is doing, where she goes, who she sees, what they talk of, everything and you will tell me.

quotes.sentence: Oh rise, rise. Let us drink to our understanding. Smile, cousin. My sister is a beautiful woman. And it's all for the good of the realm.
quotes.originalSentence: Oh rise, rise. Let us drink to our understanding. Oh, you don't have a cup. Oh, well. Smile, cousin. My sister is a beautiful woman. And it's all for the good of the realm. Go back and tell her that I beg her forgiveness, that I want no more conflict between us and that henceforth I shall do nothing without her consent.

quotes.sentence: Oh, I'll give her Pycelle.
quotes.originalSentence: Oh, I'll give her Pycelle.

quotes.sentence: Cersei can keep him as a pet if she wants, but I will not have him on the council.
quotes.originalSentence: Yes, I'll release him in the morning. Cersei can keep him as a pet if she wants, but I will not have him on the council. I could swear that I had not harmed a single hair on his head, but that would not, strictly speaking, be true.

quotes.sentence: He was, Your Grace.
quotes.originalSentence: He was, Your Grace.

quotes.sentence: Do you want to be a Queen?
quotes.originalSentence: Do you want to be a Queen?

quotes.sentence: I'm sorry about your brother, Your Grace. People grieve for him.
quotes.originalSentence: I'm sorry about your brother, Your Grace. I wanted to let you know people grieve for him.

quotes.sentence: Fools love a fool. I grieve for him as well, for the boy he was, not the man he grew to be.
quotes.originalSentence: Fools love a fool. I grieve for him as well, for the boy he was, not the man he grew to be.

quotes.sentence: Not wine, water. Girl, where are you from?
quotes.originalSentence: Not wine, water. We'll be here for some time.  Girl, where are you from?

quotes.sentence: Maidenpool, my lord.
quotes.originalSentence: Maidenpool, my lord.

quotes.sentence: And who are the Lords of Maidenpool? Remind me.
quotes.originalSentence: And who are the Lords of Maidenpool? Remind me.

quotes.sentence: House Mooton, my lord.
quotes.originalSentence: House Mooton, my lord.

quotes.sentence: And what is their sigil? A red salmon. You're a Northerner, aren't you? One more time, where are you from?
quotes.originalSentence: And what is their sigil?  A red salmon. I think a Maidenpool girl would remember that. You're a Northerner, aren't you?  Good. One more time, where are you from?

quotes.sentence: Barrowton, my lord. House Dustin. Two crossed longaxes beneath a black crown.
quotes.originalSentence: Barrowton, my lord. House Dustin. Two crossed longaxes beneath a black crown.

quotes.originalSentence: They say he rides into battle on the back of a giant direwolf. They say he can turn into a wolf himself when he wants. They say he can't be killed.

quotes.sentence: And do you believe them?
quotes.originalSentence: And do you believe them?

quotes.sentence: No, my lord. Anyone can be killed.
quotes.originalSentence: No, my lord. Anyone can be killed.

quotes.originalSentence: Once you're safely back amongst your own people, will you give me leave to go, my lady?

quotes.sentence: You mean to kill Stannis.
quotes.originalSentence: You mean to kill Stannis.

quotes.sentence: I swore a vow.
quotes.originalSentence: I swore a vow.

quotes.sentence: But Stannis has a great army around him. His own guards are sworn to keep him safe.
quotes.originalSentence: But Stannis has a great army around him. His own guards are sworn to keep him safe.

quotes.sentence: I'm as good as any of them. I should never have fled.
quotes.originalSentence: I'm as good as any of them. I should never have fled.

quotes.sentence: Renly's death was no fault of yours. You served him bravely.
quotes.originalSentence: Renly's death was no fault of yours. You served him bravely.

quotes.sentence: I only held him that once as he was dying.
quotes.originalSentence: I only held him that once as he was dying.

quotes.originalSentence: He's gone, Brienne. You serve nothing and no one by following him into the earth. Renly's enemies are Robb's enemies as well.

quotes.sentence: I do not know your son, my lady, but I could serve you. You have courage. Promise me that you will not hold me back from Stannis.
quotes.originalSentence: I do not know your son, my lady, but I could serve you if you would have me. You have courage. Not battle courage perhaps, but, I don't know, a woman's kind of courage. And I think that when the time comes, you will not hold me back. Promise me that you will not hold me back from Stannis.

quotes.sentence: When the time comes, I will not hold you back.
quotes.originalSentence: When the time comes, I will not hold you back.

quotes.sentence: Then I am yours, my lady. I will shield your back and will give my life for yours. I swear it by the Old Gods and the New.
quotes.originalSentence: Then I am yours, my lady. I will shield your back and will give my life for yours if it comes to that. I swear it by the Old Gods and the New.

quotes.sentence: I vow that you shall always have a place in my home and at my table. I swear it by the Old Gods and the New.
quotes.originalSentence: I vow that you shall always have a place in my home and at my table, and that I shall ask no service of you that might bring you dishonour. I swear it by the Old Gods and the New.

quotes.sentence: Piss on wildfire and your cock burns off.
quotes.originalSentence: I remember reading an old sailor's proverb: "Piss on wildfire and your cock burns off."

quotes.originalSentence: My companion takes issue.

quotes.originalSentence: How much do you have?

quotes.originalSentence: My friend remains unconvinced.

quotes.originalSentence: I'm afraid I have to concur with my advisor, Wisdom Hallyne. The contents of this room could lay King's Landing low. You won't be making wildfire for my sister any longer. You'll be making it for me.

quotes.originalSentence: I've taken your castle!

quotes.originalSentence: It's Prince Theon now. Get up! You have to get dressed. I've taken Winterfell. I took it. I'm occupying it. I sent men over the walls with grappling claws and ropes.

quotes.originalSentence: And he sent me back to Pyke. I'm a Greyjoy. I can't fight for Robb and my father both. Where's Hodor?

quotes.originalSentence: Find the halfwit. My men are bringing your people into the courtyard.

quotes.sentence: So you and I can go down and tell them how you've yielded Winterfell to me.
quotes.originalSentence: So you and I can go down and tell them how you've yielded Winterfell to me.

quotes.originalSentence: The castle is mine but these people are still yours. You'll yield to keep them safe. To keep them alive. That's what a good lord would do. Think carefully about what you want to say.

quotes.originalSentence: Theon.  Did you hate us the whole time?

quotes.originalSentence: You can't insult me!

quotes.originalSentence: We've had vicious kings and we've had idiot kings, but I don't know if we've ever been cursed with a vicious idiot for a king!

And when the time is right, you will be given a position fit for your talents so that you can serve your family and protect our legacy. And if you serve faithfully, you will be rewarded with a suitable wife. And I would let myself be consumed by maggots before mocking the family name and making you heir to Casterly Rock.

quotes.sentence: You, who killed your mother to come into the world? You are an ill-made, spiteful little creature... neither gods nor men will ever compel me to let you turn Casterly Rock into your whorehouse. The next whore I catch in your bed, I'll hang.
quotes.originalSentence: Why? You ask that? You, who killed your mother to come into the world? You are an ill-made, spiteful little creature full of envy, lust, and low cunning. Men's laws give you the right to bear my name and display my colors since I cannot prove that you are not mine. And to teach me humility, the gods have condemned me to watch you waddle about wearing that proud lion that was my father's sigil and his father's before him. But neither gods nor men will ever compel me to let you turn Casterly Rock into your whorehouse. Go, now. Speak no more of your rights to Casterly Rock. Go.  Oh, one more thing. The next whore I catch in your bed, I'll hang.

So I prayed to the gods "Take him away, make him die". He got the pox and I knew I was the worst woman who ever lived. A murderer. I'd condemned this poor, innocent child to a horrible death all because I was jealous of his mother, a woman he didn't even know! So I prayed to all Seven Gods "Let the boy live. quotes.sentence: He burned my parts on a brazier; the flames burned blue, and I heard a voice answer his call. Still dream of that night. Not of the sorcerer, not of his blade...I dream of the voice.
quotes.originalSentence: As a boy, in Myr, I traveled with a troupe of actors through the Free Cities. One day, a man approached my master with an offer too tempting to refuse. I feared the man meant to use me, as I'd heard some men use small boys, but what he wanted was far worse. He gave me a potion that made me powerless to move or speak, yet did nothing to dull my senses. With a hooked blade, he sliced me, root and stem, chanting all the while. He burned my parts on a brazier; the flames burned blue, and I heard a voice answer his call. Still dream of that night. Not of the sorcerer, not of his blade...I dream of the voice. Was it a god? A demon? Some conjurer's trick? I don't know, but the sorcerer called and a voice answered. And ever since that day, I have hated magic, and all who practice it. But, you can see why I was eager to aid in your fight against Stannis and his Red Priestess, a symbolic revenge, of sorts.

quotes.sentence: Yes, I feel the need for actual revenge. Against the actual person who tried to have me killed...

quotes.sentence: Influence is largely a matter of patience, I find... Influence grows, like a weed. I tended mine, patiently...
quotes.originalSentence: You do not possess at the moment. But influence is largely a matter of patience, I find. Once I had served the sorcerer's purpose, he threw me out of his house, to die. I resolved to live, to spite him. I begged, I sold what parts of my body remained to me, I became an excellent thief. And soon, learned that the contents of a man's letters are more valuable than the contents of his purse. Step by step, one distasteful task after another, I made my way from the slums of Myr to the Small Council Chamber. Influence grows, like a weed. I tended mine, patiently, until its tendrils reached from the Red Keep, all the way across to the far side of the world where I managed to wrap them around something very special.

quotes.originalSentence: Winter could last five years. Once my bannermen are home again, sitting by the fire, surrounded by their families, warm and safe, they'll never ride south again. When I gathered my Lords together, we had a purpose, a mission.  Now, we're like a band of bickering children!

quotes.originalSentence: You swore a solemn vow, remember? You're supposed to get me to King's Landing in one piece.  Not going so well, is it? No wonder Renly died with you guarding him.  That was unworthy. Forgive me. You protected me better than most.

quotes.sentence: The Mad King was obsessed with wildfire. He loved to watch people burn. He said
quotes.originalSentence: The Mad King was obsessed with it. He loved to watch people burn, the way their skin blackened and blistered and melted off their bones. He burned lords he didn't like. He burned Hands who disobeyed him. He burned anyone who was against him. Before long, half the country was against him. Aerys saw traitors everywhere. So he had his pyromancer place caches of wildfire all over the city. Beneath the Sept of Baelor and the slums of Flea Bottom. Under houses, stables, taverns. Even beneath the Red Keep itself. Finally, the day of reckoning came. Robert Baratheon marched on the capital after his victory at the Trident. But my father arrived first with the whole Lannister army at his back, promising to defend the city against the rebels. I knew my father better than that. He's never been one to pick the losing side. I told the Mad King as much. I urged him to surrender peacefully. But the king didn't listen to me. He didn't listen to Varys who tried to warn him. But he did listen to Grand Maester Pycelle, that grey, sunken cunt. "You can trust the Lannisters," he said. "The Lannisters have always been true friends of the crown." So we opened the gates and my father sacked the city. Once again, I came to the king, begging him to surrender. He told me to... bring him my father's head. Then he... turned to his pyromancer. "Burn them all," he said. "Burn them in their homes. Burn them in their beds." Tell me, if your precious Renly commanded you to kill your own father and stand by while thousands of men, women, and children burned alive, would you have done it? Would you have kept your oath then?  First, I killed the pyromancer. And then when the king turned to flee, I drove my sword into his back. "Burn them all," he kept saying. "Burn them all." I don't think he expected to die. He, he meant to... burn with the rest of us and rise again, reborn as a dragon to turn his enemies to ash. I slit his throat to make sure that didn't happen. That's where Ned Stark found me.

quotes.sentence: Why was he smashing all those beetles?
quotes.originalSentence: I was curious. Why was he smashing all those beetles? What did he get out of it? First thing I did was ask him: "Orson, why are you smashing all those beetles?" He gave me an answer: "Thmasth the beetles! Thmasth 'em! Khuun, khuun, khuun!" I wasn't deterred. I was the smartest person I knew. Certainly I had the wherewithal to unravel the mysteries that lay at the heart of a moron. So, I went to Maester Volarik's library.

quotes.sentence: Volarik. Tried to touch me once.
quotes.originalSentence: Volarik. Tried to touch me once.

quotes.sentence: Too much has been written about great men and not enough about morons.
quotes.originalSentence: Turns out, far too much has been written about great men and not nearly enough about morons. Doesn't seem right. In any case, I found nothing that illuminated the nature of Orson's affliction, or the reason behind his relentless beetle slaughter. So, I went back to the source. I may not have been able to speak with Orson, but I could observe him, watch him the way men watch animals to come to a deeper understanding of their behavior.

quotes.sentence: Did I hear dragon wings? No. I heard: Khuun, khuun, khuun.
quotes.originalSentence: And as I watched, I became more and more sure of it. There was something happening there. His face was like the page of a book written in a language I didn't understand. But he wasn't mindless. He had his reasons. And I became possessed with knowing what they were. I began to spend inordinate amounts of time watching him. I would eat my lunch in the garden, chewing my mutton to the music of "khuun, khuun, khuun..." And when I wasn't watching him, I was thinking about him. Father droned on about the family legacy and I thought about Orson's beetles. I read the histories of Targaryen conquests. Did I hear dragon wings? No. I heard: Khuun, khuun, khuun. And I still couldn't figure out why he was doing it. And I had to know, because it was horrible, that all these beetles should be dying for no reason.

quotes.sentence: Who gives a dusty fuck about a bunch of beetles?
quotes.originalSentence: Every day around the world, men, women, and children are murdered by the score. Who gives a dusty fuck about a bunch of beetles--?

quotes.sentence: In my dreams I found myself standing on a beach made of beetle husks.
quotes.originalSentence: I know, I know. But still, it filled me with dread. Piles and piles of them, years and years of them. How many countless living crawling things smashed and dried out and returned to the dirt? In my dreams I found myself standing on a beach made of beetle husks stretching as far as the eye could see. I woke up crying, weeping for their shattered little bodies. I tried to stop Orson once.
