quotes.sentence: And that little monster is out there, somewhere, drawing breath! Did you set him free? You killed him by mistake. With stupidity.
quotes.originalSentence: And now our father is dead. And that little monster is out there, somewhere, drawing breath! Did you set him free?  Tyrion may be a monster, but at least he killed our father on purpose. You killed him by mistake. With stupidity. You're a man of action, aren't you? When it occurs to you to do something, you do it. Never mind the consequences. Take a look. Look at the consequences. Here they are. He loved you more than anyone in this world.

quotes.sentence: Angry snakes lash out, makes chopping off their heads easier. Find the men who did this, and bring them to me.
quotes.originalSentence: Angry snakes lash out, makes chopping off their heads a lot easier. Find the men who did this, and bring them to me.

quotes.sentence: You never told me why you set me free.
quotes.originalSentence: You never told me why you set me free.

quotes.sentence: Your brother asked me to.
quotes.originalSentence: Your brother asked me to.

quotes.sentence: You could've said no.
quotes.originalSentence: You could've said no.

quotes.sentence: Refuse the Kingslayer? A dangerous proposition.
quotes.originalSentence: Refuse the Kingslayer? A dangerous proposition.

quotes.sentence: Not as dangerous as releasing me. You risked your life, your position, everything. Why?
quotes.originalSentence: Not as dangerous as releasing me. You risked your life, your position, everything. Why? You're not family, you owe me nothing.

quotes.sentence: I didn't do it for you. I did it for the Seven Kingdoms.
quotes.originalSentence: I didn't do it for you. I did it for the Seven Kingdoms.

quotes.sentence: A drunken dwarf will never be the savior of the Seven Kingdoms.
quotes.originalSentence: A drunken dwarf will never be the savior of the Seven Kingdoms.

quotes.sentence: I don't believe in saviors. I believe men of talent have a part to play.
quotes.originalSentence: I don't believe in saviors. I believe men of talent have a part to play in the wars to come.

quotes.sentence: I'm done with Westeros, and Westeros is done with me.
quotes.originalSentence: You're going to have to find another soldier. I'm done with Westeros, and Westeros is done with me.

quotes.sentence: Self-pity is not one of your admirable qualities. Earning power for yourself? That takes work.
quotes.originalSentence: You have many admirable qualities. Self-pity is not one of them. Any fool with a bit of luck can find himself born into power, but earning it for yourself? That takes work.

quotes.sentence: I'm not well-suited for work.
quotes.originalSentence: I'm not well-suited for work.

quotes.sentence: You have your father's instincts for politics and you have compassion.
quotes.originalSentence: I think you are. You have your father's instincts for politics and you have compassion.

quotes.sentence: Compassion? I killed my lover, I shot my own father with a crossbow!
quotes.originalSentence: Compassion? Yes, I killed my lover with my bare hands, I shot my own father with a crossbow!

quotes.sentence: I never said you were perfect.
quotes.originalSentence: I never said you were perfect.

quotes.sentence: What is it you want, exactly?
quotes.originalSentence: What is it you want, exactly?

quotes.sentence: Peace, prosperity. A land where the powerful do not prey on the powerless.
quotes.originalSentence: Peace, prosperity. A land where the powerful do not prey on the powerless.

quotes.sentence: Where castles are made of gingerbread? The powerful have always preyed on the powerless.
quotes.originalSentence: Where the castles are made of gingerbread and the moats are filled with blackberry wine? The powerful have always preyed on the powerless. That's how they became powerful in the first place.

quotes.sentence: Perhaps we've grown so accustomed to horror, we assume there's no other way.
quotes.originalSentence: Perhaps, and perhaps we've grown so accustomed to horror, we assume there's no other way.  If you sat on the Iron Throne, would you spread misery throughout the land?

quotes.sentence: I will never sit on the Iron Throne.
quotes.originalSentence: I will never sit on the Iron Throne.

quotes.sentence: You could help another take that seat. The Seven Kingdoms need someone stronger than Tommen, but gentler than Stannis.
quotes.originalSentence: No, you won't, but you could help another climb those steps, and take that seat.  The Seven Kingdoms need someone stronger than Tommen, but gentler than Stannis. A monarch who can intimidate the high Lords, and inspire the people. A ruler loved by millions, with a powerful army and the right family name.

quotes.sentence: Good luck finding him.
quotes.originalSentence: Good luck finding him.

quotes.sentence: Who said anything about him? You can drink yourself to death, or ride with me to Meereen and meet Daenerys Targaryen.
quotes.originalSentence: Who said anything about him? You have a choice, my friend. You can stay here at Illyrio's palace and drink yourself to death. Or you can ride with me to Meereen, meet Daenerys Targaryen, and decide if the world is worth fighting for.

quotes.sentence: Can I drink myself to death on the way to Meereen?
quotes.originalSentence: Can I drink myself to death on the way to Meereen?

quotes.sentence: My name is Brienne of Tarth.
quotes.originalSentence: Lord Baelish, Lady Sansa, my name is Brienne of Tarth.

quotes.sentence: Your loyalty came free of charge. Someone appears to have paid quite a bit for it since then.
quotes.originalSentence: We've met, with Renly Baratheon. What did he say about you? He said... "Your loyalty came free of charge." Someone appears to have paid quite a bit for it since then.

quotes.sentence: I was your mother's sworn sword. I gave my word I would find and protect you. I swear it by the Old Gods and the New.
quotes.originalSentence: Lady Sansa. Before your mother's death, I was her sworn sword. I gave my word I would find you and protect you. I will shield your back and give you counsel, and give my life for yours if need be. I swear it by the Old Gods and the New.

quotes.sentence: You were Catelyn Stark's sworn sword?
quotes.originalSentence: Please, Lady Brienne, no need for such formality. You were Catelyn Stark's sworn sword?

quotes.sentence: I was.
quotes.originalSentence: I was.

quotes.sentence: Strange. I knew Cat, she never mentioned you.
quotes.originalSentence: Strange. I knew Cat from the time we were children. She never mentioned you.

quotes.sentence: It was after Renly's murder.
quotes.originalSentence: It was after Renly's murder.

quotes.sentence: Ahh, yes. You were accused of killing him.
quotes.originalSentence: Ahh, yes. You were accused of killing him.

quotes.sentence: I tried to save him!
quotes.originalSentence: I tried to save him!

quotes.sentence: But you were accused.
quotes.originalSentence: But you were accused.

quotes.sentence: By men who didn't see what happened.
quotes.originalSentence: By men who didn't see what happened.

quotes.sentence: And what did happen?
quotes.originalSentence: And what did happen?

quotes.sentence: He was murdered by a shadow. A shadow with the face of Stannis Baratheon.
quotes.originalSentence: He was murdered by a shadow. A shadow with the face of Stannis Baratheon.

quotes.sentence: A shadow? With a face? She swore to protect Renly, she failed. She swore to protect your mother; she failed.
quotes.originalSentence: A shadow? With a face?  This woman swore to protect Renly, she failed. She swore to protect your mother; she failed. Why would I want someone with your history of failure guarding Lady Sansa?

quotes.sentence: Why should you have any say in her affairs?
quotes.originalSentence: Why should you have any say in her affairs?

quotes.sentence: Because I am her uncle. We're family now. You are an outsider.
quotes.originalSentence: Because I am her uncle. I married her aunt Lysa, shortly before my beloved's untimely death. We're family now. And you are an outsider. Forgive me, Lady Brienne, but experience has made me wary of outsiders.

quotes.sentence: Lady Sansa, if we can have a word alone?
quotes.originalSentence: Lady Sansa, if we can have a word alone?

quotes.sentence: Please, my Lady, if you would let me explain.
quotes.originalSentence: Please, my Lady, if you would let me explain.

quotes.sentence: I saw you at Joffrey's wedding, bowing to the King.
quotes.originalSentence: I saw you at Joffrey's wedding, bowing to the King.

quotes.sentence: Neither of us wanted to be there. Sometimes we don't have a choice.
quotes.originalSentence: Neither of us wanted to be there. Sometimes we don't have a choice.

quotes.sentence: And sometimes we do. You should leave.
quotes.originalSentence: And sometimes we do. You should leave.

quotes.sentence: Uncle Kevan! It would please the King if you would serve as his Master of War.
quotes.originalSentence: Uncle Kevan! In light of your position as Commander of the Lannister armies, it would please the King if you would serve as his Master of War. No man living better deserves the title.

quotes.sentence: The King is very busy at this moment.
quotes.originalSentence: The King is very busy at this moment.

quotes.sentence: He's asked me to speak on his behalf.
quotes.originalSentence: He is learning. On this occasion, in his capacity as ruler, he's asked me to speak on his behalf.

quotes.sentence: My brother has left the Capitol to lead a sensitive diplomatic mission.
quotes.originalSentence: My brother has left the Capitol to lead a sensitive diplomatic mission.

quotes.sentence: That is not your concern as Master of War.
quotes.originalSentence: That is not your concern as Master of War.

quotes.sentence: You would abandon your King in his time of need?
quotes.originalSentence: You would abandon your King in his time of need?

quotes.sentence: About what?
quotes.originalSentence: About what?

quotes.sentence: The "Mad King"? You're here to remind me of my enemies' lies? Consider me reminded.
quotes.originalSentence: The "Mad King"? You're here to remind me of my enemies' lies? Consider me reminded.

quotes.sentence: Go on.
quotes.originalSentence: Go on.

quotes.sentence: I'm not my father.
quotes.originalSentence: I'm not my father.

quotes.sentence: I will not have the Son of the Harpy executed without a fair trial.
quotes.originalSentence: I will not have the Son of the Harpy executed without a fair trial.

quotes.sentence: You said there was no Jaqen H'ghar here.
quotes.originalSentence: You said there was no Jaqen H'ghar here.

quotes.sentence: A man is not Jaqen H'ghar.
quotes.originalSentence: There isn't. A man is not Jaqen H'ghar.

quotes.sentence: Well, then who are you, then?
quotes.originalSentence: Well, then who are you, then?

quotes.sentence: No one. And that is who a girl must become.
quotes.originalSentence: No one. And that is who a girl must become.

quotes.sentence: We can't hold the North if we let these lesser Lords insult us.
quotes.originalSentence: We can't hold the North if we let these lesser Lords insult us.

quotes.sentence: Lord Cerwyn refused to pay... Said the Warden of the North would always be a Stark.
quotes.originalSentence: Lord Cerwyn refused to pay. Said the Warden of the North would always be a Stark, and he'd be damned if he'd kiss a traitor's boot.

quotes.sentence: I flayed him living, along with his wife and brother. Made his son watch.
quotes.originalSentence: I flayed him living, along with his wife and brother. Made his son watch.

quotes.sentence: The new Lord Cerwyn paid his taxes.
quotes.originalSentence: The new Lord Cerwyn paid his taxes.

quotes.sentence: Our pact with the Lannisters protects...
quotes.originalSentence: Our pact with the Lannisters protects...

quotes.sentence: That's Moat Cailin.
quotes.originalSentence: That's Moat Cailin.

quotes.sentence: Bit shabby, isn't it? You've been here before?
quotes.originalSentence: Yes. Bit shabby, isn't it? You've been here before?

quotes.sentence: On my way down to King's Landing... Where are you taking me?
quotes.originalSentence: On my way down to King's Landing, with my father and Arya. Where are you taking me?

quotes.sentence: The Boltons have Winterfell. Your marriage proposal, it wasn't for you.
quotes.originalSentence: The Boltons have Winterfell. Your marriage proposal, it wasn't for you.

quotes.sentence: Roose Bolton murdered my brother! He betrayed my family!
quotes.originalSentence: Roose Bolton murdered my brother! He betrayed my family!

quotes.sentence: He serves the Lannisters!
quotes.originalSentence: He serves the Lannisters!

quotes.sentence: For now.
quotes.originalSentence: For now.

quotes.sentence: I won't go.
quotes.originalSentence: I won't go.

quotes.sentence: Winterfell is your home.
quotes.originalSentence: Winterfell is your home.

quotes.sentence: Not anymore.
quotes.originalSentence: Not anymore.

quotes.sentence: Always. You're a Stark. Your place is in the North.
quotes.originalSentence: Always. You're a Stark. Dyeing your hair doesn't change that. You're Sansa Stark, eldest surviving child of Ned and Catelyn Stark. Your place is in the North.

quotes.sentence: I can't marry him. You can't make me! He's a traitor, a murderer.
quotes.originalSentence: I can't marry him. You can't make me! He's a traitor, a murderer.

quotes.sentence: You're not marrying Roose Bolton! No, you'll be marrying his son and heir, Ramsay.
quotes.originalSentence: You're not marrying Roose Bolton! No, you'll be marrying his son and heir, Ramsay. One day, he'll be Warden of the North, and you'll...

quotes.sentence: Sansa...
quotes.originalSentence: Sansa...

quotes.sentence: No, you can't make me! I will die before I have to go there.
quotes.originalSentence: No, you can't make me! I will starve myself. I will die before I have to go there.

quotes.sentence: You've been running all your life. Stop being a bystander. There's no justice in the world, not unless we make it. You loved your family. Avenge them.
quotes.originalSentence: I won't force you to do anything. Don't you know, by now, how much I care about you? Say the word, and we'll turn the horses around. But listen to me. Listen. You've been running all your life. Terrible things have happened to your family, and you weep. You sit alone in a darkened room, mourning their fates. You've been a bystander to tragedy, from the day they executed your father. Stop being a bystander, d'you hear me? Stop running. There's no justice in the world, not unless we make it. You loved your family. Avenge them.

quotes.sentence: "Brienne the Beauty", they called me... Renly Baratheon took me in his arms. "Don't let them see your tears," he told me.
quotes.originalSentence: When I was a girl, my father held a ball. I'm his only living child, so he wanted to make a good match for me. He invited dozens of young Lords to Tarth. I didn't want to go, but he dragged me to the ballroom. And it was wonderful. None of the boys noticed how mulish and tall I was. They shoved each other and threatened to duel when they thought it was their turn to dance. And whispered in my ear, how they wanted to marry me and take me back to their castles. My father smiled at me, and I smiled at him. I had never been so happy...'till I saw a few of the boys snickering. And then, they all started to laugh; they couldn't keep the game going any longer. They were toying with me. "Brienne the Beauty", they called me. Great joke. And I realized I was the ugliest girl alive. A great, lumbering beast. I tried to run away, but Renly Baratheon took me in his arms. "Don't let them see your tears," he told me, "They're nasty little shits, and nasty little shits aren't worth crying over." He danced with me, and none of the other boys could say a word. Renly was the King's brother, after all.

quotes.sentence: He saved me from being a joke... And I couldn't save him. One day, I will avenge King Renly.
quotes.originalSentence: Yes, Pod, he liked men! I'm not an idiot. He didn't love me, he didn't want me. He danced with me because he was kind, and didn't want to see me hurt. He saved me from being a joke... from that day, until his last day. And I couldn't save him, in return. Nothing's more hateful than failing to protect the one you love. One day, I will avenge King Renly.

quotes.sentence: Stannis is a man, not a shadow, and a man can be killed.
quotes.originalSentence: A shadow with the face of Stannis Baratheon. I know it was Stannis. I know it in my heart. Stannis is a man, not a shadow, and a man can be killed.

quotes.sentence: Ser Alliser... I name you First Ranger. Lord Janos... I'm giving you command of Greyguard.
quotes.originalSentence: Ser Alliser. You have more experience than any other Ranger at Castle Black. You proved your valor many times over, while defending the Wall from the Wildling attack. I name you First Ranger. Lord Janos.  I'm giving you command of Greyguard.

quotes.sentence: The fort is in a sorry state. Restore it as best you can.
quotes.originalSentence: Yes, the fort is in a sorry state. Restore it as best you can. First builder Yarwick can spare ten men.

quotes.sentence: All right, all right! Enough of that!
quotes.originalSentence: All right, all right! Enough of that!

quotes.sentence: You mistake me, my Lord. That was a command, not an offer. Ride for Greyguard.
quotes.originalSentence: You mistake me, my Lord. That was a command, not an offer. Pack your arms and armor, say your farewells, and ride for Greyguard.

quotes.sentence: Are you refusing to obey my order?
quotes.originalSentence: Are you refusing to obey my order?

quotes.sentence: Take Lord Janos outside. Olly, bring me my sword.
quotes.originalSentence: Take Lord Janos outside. Olly, bring me my sword.

quotes.sentence: A young man said I'd find the High Sparrow back here. Where is he?
quotes.originalSentence: A young man said I'd find the High Sparrow back here. Where is he?

quotes.sentence: Why no shoes?
quotes.originalSentence: Why no shoes?

quotes.sentence: Is that why you came to King's Landing? To remind everyone?
quotes.originalSentence: Is that why you came to King's Landing? To remind everyone?

quotes.sentence: Perhaps they're right.
quotes.originalSentence: Perhaps they're right.

quotes.sentence: An unacceptable way to treat the chosen representative of the gods... wouldn't you say?
quotes.originalSentence: An unacceptable way to treat the chosen representative of the gods in this world, wouldn't you say?

quotes.sentence: The High Septon wants me to execute you.
quotes.originalSentence: The High Septon came to speak to me today. He doesn't want me to arrest you. He wants me to execute you.

quotes.sentence: The faith and the crown are the two pillars that hold up this world. We must do everything necessary to protect one another.
quotes.originalSentence: My thoughts on the matter are in line with your own. The High Septon's behavior was corrosive, as was his attitude. Having a man like that reside in the sept eats away at the faith from the inside. So now he resides in the Red Keep dungeons instead. The faith and the crown are the two pillars that hold up this world. One collapses, so does the other. We must do everything necessary to protect one another.

quotes.sentence: Are you lonely?
quotes.originalSentence: Are you lonely?

quotes.sentence: Just bored.
quotes.originalSentence: Just bored.

quotes.sentence: My father used to tell me that boredom indicates a lack of inner resources.
quotes.originalSentence: My father used to tell me that boredom indicates a lack of inner resources.

quotes.sentence: Were you bored a lot, too?
quotes.originalSentence: Were you bored a lot, too?

quotes.sentence: I know Castle Black is no place for a child, but I...
quotes.originalSentence: I know Castle Black is no place for a child, but I...

quotes.sentence: I like it. I know Mother didn't want to bring me.
quotes.originalSentence: I like it. I thought I'd be left at home. I know Mother didn't want to bring me.

quotes.sentence: Why do you say that?
quotes.originalSentence: Why do you say that?

quotes.sentence: She told me, "I don't want to bring you."
quotes.originalSentence: She told me, "I don't want to bring you."

quotes.sentence: She shouldn't have said that.
quotes.originalSentence: She shouldn't have said that.

quotes.sentence: Are you ashamed of me, Father?
quotes.originalSentence: Are you ashamed of me, Father?

quotes.sentence: You are the Princess Shireen of House Baratheon. And you are my daughter.
quotes.originalSentence: When you were an infant, a Dornish trader landed on Dragonstone. His goods were junk except for one wooden doll. He'd even sewn a dress on it in the colors of our House. No doubt he'd heard of your birth and assumed new fathers were easy targets. I still remember how you smiled when I put that doll in your cradle. How you pressed it to your cheek. By the time we burnt the doll, it was too late. I was told you would die. Or worse, the grayscale would go slow. Let you grow just enough to know the world before taking it away from you. Everyone advised me to send you to the ruins of Valyria to live out your short life with the Stone Men, before the sickness spread to the castle. I told them all to go to hell. I called in every maester on this side of the world. Every healer, every apothecary. They stopped the disease and saved your life. Because you did not belong across the world with the bloody Stone Men. You are the Princess Shireen of House Baratheon. And you are my daughter.

quotes.sentence: Lord Commander.
quotes.originalSentence: Lord Commander.

quotes.sentence: How can I help you?
quotes.originalSentence: How can I help you?

quotes.sentence: Winterfell was your home once. Don't you want to chase the rats out of it?
quotes.originalSentence: Come with us when we ride south. None of us know the castle as well as you do. Its hidden tunnels, its weaknesses, its people. Winterfell was your home once. Don't you want to chase the rats out of it?

quotes.sentence: Castle Black is my home now. The Night's Watch take no part in the wars of the Seven Kingdoms.
quotes.originalSentence: Castle Black is my home now. The Night's Watch take no part in the wars of the Seven Kingdoms.

quotes.sentence: There's only one war. Life against death.
quotes.originalSentence: There's only one war. Life against death. Come. Let me show you what you're fighting for.

quotes.sentence: You're gonna show me some visions in the fire. Forgive me my lady, I don't trust in visions.
quotes.originalSentence: You're gonna show me some visions in the fire. Forgive me my lady, I don't trust in visions.

quotes.sentence: No visions. No magic. Just life. There's power in you, you resist it and that's your mistake. Embrace it.
quotes.originalSentence: No visions. No magic. Just life.  Do you feel my heart beating? There's power in you, you resist it and that's your mistake. Embrace it. The Lord of Light made us male and female. Two parts of a greater whole. You're not joining this power. Power to make life, power to make light, and power to cast shadows.

quotes.sentence: I don't think Stannis would like that very much.
quotes.originalSentence: I don't think Stannis would like that very much.

quotes.sentence: Then we shouldn't tell him.
quotes.originalSentence: Then we shouldn't tell him.

quotes.sentence: I can't.
quotes.originalSentence: I can't.

quotes.sentence: I swore a vow. I loved another.
quotes.originalSentence: I swore a vow. I loved another.

quotes.sentence: The dead don't need lovers. Only the living.
quotes.originalSentence: The dead don't need lovers. Only the living.

quotes.sentence: I know. But I still love her.
quotes.originalSentence: I know. But I still love her.

quotes.sentence: You know nothing, Jon Snow.
quotes.originalSentence: "You know nothing, Jon Snow."

quotes.sentence: I thought I might find you here. Your aunt Lyanna.
quotes.originalSentence: I thought I might find you here. Your aunt Lyanna.

quotes.sentence: Father never talked about her. But sometimes I'd find him down here lighting the candles. He'd say she was beautiful.
quotes.originalSentence: Father never talked about her. But sometimes I'd find him down here lighting the candles. He'd say she was beautiful.

quotes.sentence: He rode right past his wife Elia Martell, and all the smiles died. He lay a crown of winter roses in Lyanna's lap. How many tens of thousands had to die because Rhaegar chose your aunt?
quotes.originalSentence: I saw her once. I was a boy living with your mother's family. Lord Whent had a great tourney at Harrenhall. Everyone was there. The Mad King, your father, Robert Baratheon. And Lyanna...she was already promised to Robert. You can imagine what it was like for me, a boy from nowhere with nothing to his name, watching these legendary men tilting at the lists. The last two riders were Barristan Selmy and Rhaegar Targaryen. When Rhaegar won, everyone cheered for their prince. I remember the girls laughing when he took off his helmet and saw that silver hair. How handsome he was. Until he rode right past his wife Elia Martell, and all the smiles died. I had never seen so many people so quiet. He rode past his wife and he lay a crown of winter roses in Lyanna's lap. Blue with frost. How many tens of thousands had to die because Rhaegar chose your aunt?

quotes.sentence: Yes he chose her. And then he kidnapped her and raped her.
quotes.originalSentence: Yes he chose her. And then he kidnapped her and raped her.

quotes.sentence: Come. Let's speak somewhere the dead can't hear us.
quotes.originalSentence: Come. Let's speak somewhere the dead can't hear us.

quotes.sentence: This ship's sailing to Oldtown. We'll get off earlier, not far from Sunspear.
quotes.originalSentence: This ship's sailing to Oldtown. We'll get off earlier, not far from Sunspear. Row ourselves to the Dornish shores in the night.

quotes.sentence: You should be happy to go back then.
quotes.originalSentence: You should be happy to go back then.

quotes.sentence: We are not kidnapping their princess. We are rescuing my niece, bringing her back to the family.
quotes.originalSentence: We are not kidnapping their princess. We are rescuing my niece, bringing her back to the family.

quotes.sentence: Kill the boy, Jon Snow. Winter is almost upon us. Kill the boy and let the man be born.
quotes.originalSentence: You will find little joy in your command. But, with luck, you’ll find the strength to do what needs to be done. Kill the boy, Jon Snow. Winter is almost upon us. Kill the boy and let the man be born.

quotes.sentence: He's not Theon Greyjoy anymore. He's a new man. Aren't you, Reek?
quotes.originalSentence: I heard you two had been reunited. A fitting place for it. I like to imagine the last time you spoke was in this very room. Are you still angry with him after he...what he did? Don't worry. The North remembers. I punished him for it. He's not Ironborn anymore. He's not Theon Greyjoy anymore. He's a new man. A new person anyway. Aren't you, Reek?

quotes.sentence: Yes Master.
quotes.originalSentence: Yes Master.

quotes.sentence: That's his new name. Reek.
quotes.originalSentence: That's his new name. Reek.

quotes.sentence: Why are you doing this?
quotes.originalSentence: Why are you doing this?

quotes.sentence: Because Reek has something to say to you. Don't you, Reek?
quotes.originalSentence: Because Reek has something to say to you. Don't you, Reek?

quotes.sentence: Apologize to Lady Sansa for what you did. Apologize for murdering her two brothers.
quotes.originalSentence: An apology? Apologize to Lady Sansa for what you did. Apologize for murdering her two brothers.

quotes.sentence: I'm sorry.
quotes.originalSentence: I'm sorry.

quotes.sentence: An apology doesn't mean anything if you're not looking the person in the eye.
quotes.originalSentence: Look at her, Reek. An apology doesn't mean anything if you're not looking the person in the eye.

quotes.sentence: Sorry about what?
quotes.originalSentence: Sorry about what?

quotes.sentence: Killing your brothers.
quotes.originalSentence: Killing your brothers.

quotes.sentence: Reek here is the nearest thing to living kin that you have left. Reek! You will give away the bride.
quotes.originalSentence: There! Over and done with it. Doesn't everyone feel better? I do.  That was getting very tense.  You know what, my lady? What with him having murdered your brothers and the rest of your family gone, Reek here is the nearest thing to living kin that you have left. Reek! You will give away the bride. Someone has to. What better person? Good?  Good?

quotes.sentence: Wonderful!
quotes.originalSentence: Wonderful!

quotes.sentence: Very happy for you.
quotes.originalSentence: Very happy for you.

quotes.sentence: How can you be sure?
quotes.originalSentence: How can you be sure?

quotes.sentence: That she's pregnant. I mean...how can you tell?

You should hold on to your candles. The nights are so long now.

quotes.sentence: She had no wealth, no lands, no army, only a name... then she acquired all three, along with three dragons.
quotes.originalSentence: When I was a young man, I heard a story about a baby born during the worst storm in living memory. She had no wealth, no lands, no army, only a name and a handful of supporters, most of whom probably thought they could use that name to benefit themselves. They kept her alive, moving her from place to place, often hours ahead of the men who'd been sent to kill her. She was eventually sold off to some warlord on the edge of the world and that appeared to be that. And then a few years later, the most well-informed person I knew told me that this girl without wealth, lands, or armies had somehow acquired all three in a very short span of time, along with three dragons. He thought she was our best, last chance to build a better world. I thought you were worth meeting at the very least.

quotes.sentence: And why are you worth meeting? Why should I spend my time listening to you?
quotes.originalSentence: And why are you worth meeting? Why should I spend my time listening to you?

quotes.sentence: You cannot build a better world on your own. You have no one at your side who understands the land you want to rule.
quotes.originalSentence: Because you cannot build a better world on your own. You have no one at your side who understands the land you want to rule. The strengths and weaknesses of the Houses that will either join or oppose you.

quotes.originalSentence: I will have a very large army and very large dragons.

quotes.sentence: Killing and politics aren't always the same thing. I could do an even better job advising a ruler worth the name. If that is indeed what you are.
quotes.originalSentence: Killing and politics aren't always the same thing. When I served as Hand of the King, I did quite well with the latter considering the king in question preferred torturing animals to leading his people. I could do an even better job advising a ruler worth the name. If that is indeed what you are.

quotes.originalSentence: Why should the people trust a queen who can't keep her promises?

quotes.originalSentence: Whomever Ser Jorah was when he started informing on you, he is no longer that man. I can't remember ever seeing a sane man as devoted to anything as he is to serving you. He claims he would kill for you and die for you and nothing I ever witnessed gives me reason to doubt him. And yet he did betray you. Did he have an opportunity to confess his betrayal?

quotes.originalSentence: I was helping you. You wanted to escape. There is no escape. Not ever. Theon Greyjoy tried to escape. The master knew. He knows everything. He hunted him. He caught him, strapped him to a cross, cut away piece after piece until there was no Theon left.

Ruling, without the rich? House Targaryen is gone. Not a single person who shares your blood is alive to support you. The Starks are gone, as well; our two terrible fathers saw to that. The remaining members of House Lannister will never back you, not ever. Stannis Baratheon won't back you, either; his entire claim to the throne rests on the illegitimacy of yours. quotes.sentence: My dear, you've been stripped of dignity, publicly shamed. What's left to work with?
quotes.originalSentence: My dear, you have been stripped of your dignity and authority, publicly shamed, and confined to the Red Keep. What's left to work with?

quotes.sentence: Cersei is the mother of the King. She has his ear. The King speaks with the High Sparrow about Margaery and Loras.
quotes.originalSentence: Cersei is the mother of the King. She has the King's ear and his trust. And, the King has been speaking regularly with the High Sparrow about Queen Margaery, and Ser Loras.

quotes.sentence: The High Sparrow seized power, we bickered. Now, the Seven Kingdoms rests in his dirty hands. Margaery will make her walk of atonement.
quotes.originalSentence: The High Sparrow seized power, knowing full well we'd bicker among ourselves, instead of seizing it back. Here we are. Well done to us. Now, the future of the Seven Kingdoms rests in his dirty peasant hands. In a few days, he'll have a trial for me but, before that, Queen Margaery will make her walk of atonement. Yes. Margaery will repent her sins before the good people of the city.

quotes.sentence: Ohhh, no. That cannot happen. That will not happen.
quotes.originalSentence: Ohhh, no. That cannot happen. That will not happen.

quotes.originalSentence: You've got the second largest army in Westeros. You will bring them into the city, stop Queen Margaery's humiliation before it starts, and take her back into Crown custody.

quotes.sentence: You will take no action. When Tyrell armies come, you will stand down. Forbidden from standing down?
quotes.originalSentence: You will take no action at all. When the Tyrell armies come, you will stand down. Were you expressly forbidden from standing down?

quotes.sentence: It will be over before anyone can call you. When the High Sparrow's dead, and Margaery's back, do you think the King will be angry?
quotes.originalSentence: The whole thing will be over before anyone can call upon you to do anything.  When the High Sparrow's back in custody or dead, preferably, and Margaery's back at Tommen's side, do you think the King will be angry at the outcome?

quotes.originalSentence: You hate these fanatics as much as we do. You hate what they've done to your son. Do you want Lancel back? Or have you given him up for good?

quotes.sentence: Then, stand aside. And let the people that took him from you be destroyed.
quotes.originalSentence: Then, stand aside. And let the people that took him from you be destroyed.

quotes.originalSentence: They owe you their lives.  Think they'll be safe here if Roose Bolton remains Warden of the North?

quotes.originalSentence: If you don't take back the North, we'll never be safe. I want you to help me, but I'll do it myself if I have to.

quotes.sentence: Winterfell is mine, bastard. Come and see. Your brother Rickon is in my dungeon. His direwolf's skin is on my floor. Come and see.
quotes.originalSentence: "To the traitor and bastard Jon Snow, you allowed thousands of wildlings past the Wall, you have betrayed your own kind, you have betrayed the North. Winterfell is mine, bastard. Come and see. Your brother Rickon is in my dungeon. His direwolf's skin is on my floor. Come and see. I want my bride back. Send her to me, bastard, and I will not trouble you or your wildling lovers. Keep her from me, and I will ride North and slaughter every wildling man, woman, and babe living under your protection. You will watch as I skin them living."

quotes.originalSentence: It's just more of the same.

quotes.sentence: You will watch as my soldiers rape your sister, you will watch as my dogs devour your brother. Come and see.
quotes.originalSentence: "You will watch as my soldiers take turns raping your sister, you will watch as my dogs devour your wild little brother, then I will spoon your eyes from their sockets and let my dogs do the rest. Come and see. Ramsay Bolton, Lord of Winterfell and Warden of the North."

quotes.originalSentence: Lord of Winterfell and Warden of the North?

quotes.originalSentence: And here, now, what great matters do the Great Khals discuss? Which little villages you'll raid, how many girls you'll get to fuck, how many horses you'll demand in tribute. You are small men. None of you are fit to lead the Dothraki. But I am. So I will.

quotes.originalSentence: I rode north with the knights of the Vale to come to your aid. They're encamped at Moat Cailin as we speak.

He never hurt my face. He needed my face; the face of Ned Stark's daughter. But the rest of me, he did what he liked with the rest of me as long as I could still give him an heir. I don't mean, "In my tender heart, it still pains me so." I can still feel what he did in my body standing here right now.

quotes.originalSentence: You want me to beg for your life? If that's what you want, I will. Whatever you ask that is in my power, I will do.

quotes.sentence: You freed me from the monsters who murdered my family and gave me to other monsters who murdered my family. I never want to see you again.
quotes.originalSentence: You freed me from the monsters who murdered my family and gave me to other monsters who murdered my family. Go back to Moat Cailin. My brother and I will take back the North on our own. I never want to see you again.

quotes.sentence: The Blackfish has retaken Riverrun. You might consider seeking him out.
quotes.originalSentence: I would do anything to undo what's been done to you. I know that I can't. Would you allow me to say one more thing before I go? Your great uncle, Brynden, the Blackfish, has gathered what remains of the Tully forces and retaken Riverrun. You might consider seeking him out. The time may come when you need an army loyal to you.

quotes.sentence: I have an army.
quotes.originalSentence: I have an army.

quotes.sentence: Your brother's army. Half-brother.
quotes.originalSentence: Your brother's army. Half-brother.

quotes.sentence: She is a reaver, she is a warrior, she is ironborn! We will find no better leader! This is our queen.
quotes.originalSentence: I am Theon Greyjoy, last living son of Balon Greyjoy. And she is your rightful ruler. Those of you that have sailed under her, and there are many of you here, you know what she is. She is a reaver, she is a warrior, she is ironborn! We will find no better leader! This is our queen.

quotes.sentence: You weren't here. Last I heard you were galavanting around the world.
quotes.originalSentence: That would've been hard to do. You weren't here. Last I heard you were galavanting around the world having a grand ol' time.

quotes.sentence: You were gone. Yara was here. Being ironborn, leading ironborn.
quotes.originalSentence: You were gone. Yara was here. Being ironborn, leading ironborn, getting ready to bring us back to glory.

quotes.sentence: Sorry to interrupt. We're here for Queen Margaery and Ser Loras Tyrell.
quotes.originalSentence: Sorry to interrupt. We're here for Queen Margaery and Ser Loras Tyrell. Give them to us, and we'll be on our way.

quotes.sentence: I speak for King Tommen of House Baratheon, first of his name.
quotes.originalSentence: I speak for King Tommen of House Baratheon, first of his name.

quotes.sentence: Every last Sparrow will die before Margaery Tyrell walks down that street.
quotes.originalSentence: You've already insulted one Great House. It won't happen twice. Every last Sparrow will die before Margaery Tyrell walks down that street.

quotes.sentence: The Crown and the Faith are the twin pillars on which the world rests.
quotes.originalSentence: The Crown and the Faith are the twin pillars on which the world rests. Together, we will restore the Seven Kingdoms to glory.

quotes.sentence: He's beaten us! That's what happening.
quotes.originalSentence: He's beaten us! That's what happening.

quotes.sentence: You've lost it?!
quotes.originalSentence: You've lost it?!

quotes.sentence: Yes father.
quotes.originalSentence: Yes father.

quotes.sentence: It's a castle, not a bloody sheep. You didn't lose Riverrun: you let the Blackfish take it from you!
quotes.originalSentence: It's a castle, not a bloody sheep. Presumably you still know where it is? You didn't lose Riverrun: you let the Blackfish take it from you!

quotes.sentence: You did lose the Blackfish after the Red Wedding. Now he's come back and taken Riverrun!
quotes.originalSentence: You did lose the Blackfish after the Red Wedding. You had him right here in this hall and you let him leave. And when I told you to hunt him down and kill him, you couldn't find him! That's what it means to lose something! Now he's come back and taken Riverrun!

quotes.sentence: I don't think it's fair to blame us.
quotes.originalSentence: I don't think it's fair to blame us.

quotes.sentence: Riverrun is ours! Take it back!
quotes.originalSentence: For three hundred years, we kissed Tully boots, swore oaths to them and their stinking fish banners! Not again! Riverrun is ours! Take it back!

quotes.sentence: We've got ten times as many men as the damned Blackfish!
quotes.originalSentence: We've got ten times as many men as the damned Blackfish!

quotes.sentence: The Mallisters have risen against us, and the Blackwoods!
quotes.originalSentence: The Mallisters have risen against us, and the Blackwoods!

quotes.sentence: Riverrun can withstand a siege for a year!
quotes.originalSentence: Riverrun can withstand a siege for a year!

quotes.sentence: They're laughing at us! I'm not dead yet -unfortunately for you! - and I'll not leave this world until they all choke on that laughter! Take that castle back!
quotes.originalSentence: If I want excuses, I'll put her in charge! They're laughing at us! All across the Riverlands, right down to King's Landing, they're laughing at us! I hear it in my sleep! I'm not dead yet -unfortunately for you! - and I'll not leave this world until they all choke on that laughter! Take that castle back!

quotes.sentence: You'll remind him who it was that got married at the Red Wedding in the first place! His nephew. Cheer up, Lord Edmure. You're going home.
quotes.originalSentence: Oh, he'll yield. You'll show him the knife you used to kill Robb Stark's child in its whore mother's belly and you'll show him the knife you used to open his niece's throat. And you'll remind him who it was that got married at the Red Wedding in the first place! His nephew.  Cheer up, Lord Edmure. You're going home.

quotes.sentence: But I am not a khal. I will not choose three bloodriders. I choose you all! Will you give me the Seven Kingdoms? Are you with me? Now and always?
quotes.originalSentence: Every khal who ever lived chose three bloodriders, to fight beside him and guard his way. But I am not a khal. I will not choose three bloodriders. I choose you all! I will ask more of you than any khal ever asked of his khalasar! Will you ride the wooden horses across the black salt sea? Will you kill my enemies in their iron suits and tear down their stone houses?  Will you give me the Seven Kingdoms, the gift Khal Drogo promised me before the Mother of Mountains? Are you with me? Now and always?

quotes.sentence: Blackfish.
quotes.originalSentence: Blackfish.

quotes.sentence: I don't have them.
quotes.originalSentence: I don't have them.

quotes.sentence: This castle belongs to House Frey. In the name of King Tommen, I order you to surrender.
quotes.originalSentence: You know why I'm here. This castle belongs to House Frey. You're trespassing. In the name of King Tommen, I order you to surrender or...

quotes.sentence: It won't stop with Edmure. You'll force me to storm the castle.
quotes.originalSentence: It won't stop with Edmure. You'll force me to storm the castle. Hundreds will die.

quotes.sentence: If you surrender, I'll spare the lives of your men. On my honor.

Because Jeor Mormont and Jon Snow both understood that the real war isn't between a few squabbling houses. It's between the living and the dead, and make no mistake, my lady: the dead are coming.

quotes.originalSentence: As long as the Boltons hold Winterfell, the North is divided, and a divided North won't stand a chance against the Night King.  You want to protect your people, my lady, I understand. But there's no hiding from this. We have to fight and we need to do it together.

quotes.originalSentence: Grey Worm?  Right. Three lords walk into a tavern⁠—a Stark, a Martell, and a Lannister. They order ale, but when the barkeep brings them over, each of them finds a fly in his cup. The Lannister, outraged, shoves the cup aside and demands another. The Martell plucks the fly out and swallows it whole. The Stark reaches into his cup, pulls out the fly and shouts, "Spit it out, you wee shit. Spit it out."  It's funnier in Westeros.

Your son doesn't matter to me. The people in the castle don't matter to me...only Cersei. And if I have to slaughter every Tully who ever lived to get back to her, that's what I'll do.

quotes.originalSentence: Well it looks like all of them.

quotes.sentence: I invited Euron Greyjoy, the new King of the Iron Islands.
quotes.originalSentence: I invited Euron Greyjoy, the new King of the Iron Islands. You said yourself we need stronger, better allies. There you are.

quotes.sentence: How are they better allies? They both broke their promises.
quotes.originalSentence: How are they better allies? How are they different from the Freys? They both broke their promises and murdered their former friends as soon as it suited them.

quotes.sentence: So does everyone, when it suits them. They have ships, and they're good at killing.
quotes.originalSentence: So does everyone, when it suits them. Unlike the Freys, they have ships, and they're good at killing.

quotes.sentence: They're bitter, angry little people. All they know how to do is steal.
quotes.originalSentence: They're not good at anything. I know the Ironborn. They're bitter, angry little people. All they know how to do is steal the things they can't build or grow themselves.

quotes.sentence: Euron Greyjoy didn't come here for that.
quotes.originalSentence: Euron Greyjoy didn't come here for that.

quotes.sentence: What did he come here for, then?
quotes.originalSentence: What did he come here for, then?

quotes.sentence: A queen.
quotes.originalSentence: A queen.

quotes.sentence: You're not a rightful monarch though, are you?
quotes.originalSentence: You're not a rightful monarch though, are you? The Greyjoys rebelled against the throne for the right to be monarchs, but as I recall, you were soundly defeated. Come to think of it, weren't you the one who started that rebellion by sailing to Casterly Rock and burning the Lannister fleet? You certainly caught us there. Very smart move on your part. Of course, we all made it to the Iron Islands anyway. I was there.

quotes.sentence: The people I was cutting down were your own kin!
quotes.originalSentence: The people I was cutting down were your own kin!

quotes.sentence: And I enjoyed killing Greyjoys.
quotes.originalSentence: And I enjoyed killing Greyjoys.

quotes.sentence: If not the most humble.
quotes.originalSentence: If not the most humble.

quotes.sentence: What do you want in return?
quotes.originalSentence: What do you want in return?

quotes.sentence: I decline your proposal.
quotes.originalSentence: I decline your proposal.

quotes.sentence: You're not trustworthy. You murdered your own brother.
quotes.originalSentence: You're not trustworthy. You've broken promises to allies before and murdered them at the nearest opportunity. You murdered your own brother.

quotes.sentence: You served my father, didn't you, Lord Varys?
quotes.originalSentence: You served my father, didn't you, Lord Varys?

quotes.sentence: And then you served the man who overthrew him?
quotes.originalSentence: And then you served the man who overthrew him?

quotes.sentence: I had a choice, Your Grace. Serve Robert, or face the headsman's ax.
quotes.originalSentence: I had a choice, Your Grace. Serve Robert Baratheon, or face the headsman's ax.

quotes.sentence: But you didn't serve him long. You turned against him.
quotes.originalSentence: But you didn't serve him long. You turned against him.

quotes.sentence: Robert was an improvement on your father, to be sure. He simply had no interest in being King.
quotes.originalSentence: Robert was an improvement on your father, to be sure. There have been few rulers in history as cruel as the Mad King. Robert was neither mad nor cruel; he simply had no interest in being King.

quotes.sentence: So, you took it upon yourself to find a better one?
quotes.originalSentence: So, you took it upon yourself to find a better one?

quotes.sentence: When I was ready to drink myself into a small coffin, Lord Varys told me about a queen in the East.
quotes.originalSentence: Your Grace, when I was ready to drink myself into a small coffin, Lord Varys told me about a queen in the East...

quotes.sentence: Before I came to power, you favored my brother. Did your little birds tell you Viserys was cruel, stupid and weak?
quotes.originalSentence: Before I came to power, you favored my brother. All your spies, your little birds, did they tell you Viserys was cruel, stupid and weak? Would those qualities have made for a good King, in your learned opinion?

quotes.sentence: Until your marriage to Khal Drogo, Your Grace, I knew nothing about you.
quotes.originalSentence: Until your marriage to Khal Drogo, Your Grace, I knew nothing about you, save your existence and that you were said to be beautiful.

quotes.sentence: So, you and your friends traded me, like a prize horse, to the Dothraki?
quotes.originalSentence: So, you and your friends traded me, like a prize horse, to the Dothraki?

quotes.sentence: Which you turned to your advantage.
quotes.originalSentence: Which you turned to your advantage.

quotes.sentence: Who gave the order to kill me?
quotes.originalSentence: Who gave the order to kill me?

quotes.sentence: King Robert.
quotes.originalSentence: King Robert.

quotes.sentence: Who hired the assassins? Who sent word to Essos to murder Daenerys Targaryen?
quotes.originalSentence: Who hired the assassins? Who sent word to Essos to murder Daenerys Targaryen?

quotes.sentence: Your Grace, I did what had to be done...
quotes.originalSentence: Your Grace, I did what had to be done...

quotes.sentence: To keep yourself alive?
quotes.originalSentence: To keep yourself alive?

quotes.sentence: Lord Varys has proven himself a loyal servant.
quotes.originalSentence: Lord Varys has proven himself a loyal servant.

quotes.sentence: "Proven himself loyal?" Quite the opposite. What kind of a servant is that?
quotes.originalSentence: "Proven himself loyal?" Quite the opposite. If he dislikes one monarch, he conspires to crown the next one. What kind of a servant is that?

quotes.sentence: Not with any King or Queen, but with the people. The people whose hearts you aim to win!
quotes.originalSentence: The kind the Realm needs. Incompetence should not be rewarded with blind loyalty. As long as I have my eyes, I'll use them. I wasn't born into a Great House. I came from nothing. I was sold as a slave, and carved up as an offering. When I was a child I lived in alleys, gutters, abandoned houses. You wish to know where my loyalties lie? Not with any King or Queen, but with the people. The people who suffer under despots and prosper under just rule. The people whose hearts you aim to win! If you demand blind allegiance... I respect your wishes. Grey Worm can behead me, or your dragons can devour me. But if you let me live, I will serve you well; I will dedicate myself to seeing you on the Iron Throne, because I choose you. Because I know that the people have no better chance than you.

quotes.sentence: Swear this to me, Varys. You'll look me in the eye, and you'll tell me how I am failing them.
quotes.originalSentence: Swear this to me, Varys. If you ever think I'm failing the people, you won't conspire behind my back. You'll look me in the eye, as you have done today, and you'll tell me how I am failing them.

quotes.sentence: I swear it, my Queen.
quotes.originalSentence: I swear it, my Queen.

quotes.sentence: And I swear this: if you ever betray me, I'll burn you alive.
quotes.originalSentence: And I swear this: if you ever betray me, I'll burn you alive.

quotes.sentence: I would expect nothing less from the Mother of Dragons.
quotes.originalSentence: I would expect nothing less from the Mother of Dragons.

quotes.sentence: Thank you for coming. The other lords of the Reach look to you for guidance.
quotes.originalSentence: Thank you for coming. The other lords of the Reach look to you for guidance now more than ever; they might not have come if you hadn't.

quotes.sentence: You ride for Horn Hill today?
quotes.originalSentence: You ride for Horn Hill today?

quotes.sentence: Which side will you be fighting for? You were the only man to defeat Robert Baratheon.
quotes.originalSentence: And which side will you be fighting for? You were the only man to defeat Robert Baratheon in battle. Not even Rhaegar Targaryen could...

quotes.sentence: I want you to be my ranking general. I want you to swear allegiance to Cersei.
quotes.originalSentence: I want you to be my ranking general in the wars to come. I want you to swear allegiance to Cersei, and I want you to help me destroy her enemies. All her enemies.

quotes.sentence: You swore an oath to the Crown as well, Lord Tarly.
quotes.originalSentence: You swore an oath to the Crown as well, Lord Tarly.

quotes.sentence: She was a great woman once. Now, she's broken. Do you fight with us, or with the foreign savages and eunuchs?
quotes.originalSentence: She was a great woman once. Now, she's broken. She wants revenge so badly, she brought the Dothraki to our shores. The Dothraki. In Westeros, for the first time in history. I know you don't like my sister, but you have to make a choice. Do you fight with us, or with the foreign savages and eunuchs? When the war is won, the Queen will need a new Warden of the South. I can think of no better man than Randyll Tarly.

quotes.sentence: If we turn the dragons loose, tens of thousands will die.
quotes.originalSentence: If we turn the dragons loose, tens of thousands will die in the firestorms.

quotes.sentence: We don't poison little girls here. Myrcella was innocent.
quotes.originalSentence: I know how you wage war. We don't poison little girls here. Myrcella was innocent.

quotes.sentence: I am not here to be queen of the ashes.
quotes.originalSentence: That's enough! Lord Tyrion is Hand of the Queen; you will treat him with respect. I am not here to be queen of the ashes.

quotes.sentence: They won't obey you unless they fear you.
quotes.originalSentence: That's very nice to hear. Of course, I can't remember a queen who was better loved than my granddaughter. The common people loved her; the nobles loved her. And what is left of her now? Ashes. Commoners, nobles, they're all just children, really. They won't obey you unless they fear you.

quotes.sentence: She will pay for what she's done. And we will bring peace back to Westeros.
quotes.originalSentence: I realize you're here out of hatred for Cersei, and not love for me. But I swear to you, she will pay for what she's done.  And we will bring peace back to Westeros.

quotes.sentence: Peace never lasts, my dear. The Lords of Westeros are sheep. Are you a sheep? No, you're a dragon. Be a dragon.
quotes.originalSentence: Peace. Do you think that's what we had under your father? Or his father, or his? Peace never lasts, my dear. Will you take a bit of advice from an old woman?  He's a clever man, your Hand. I've known a great many clever men. I've outlived them all. You know why? I ignored them. The Lords of Westeros are sheep. Are you a sheep?  No, you're a dragon. Be a dragon.

quotes.sentence: The North is my home! I will never stop fighting for it. We can never hope to defeat the Army of the Dead alone. We need allies.
quotes.originalSentence: You all crowned me your King. I never wanted it. I never asked for it. But I accepted it because the North is my home! It's part of me, and I will never stop fighting for it, no matter the odds! But the odds are against us. None of you have seen the Army of the Dead. None of you.  We can never hope to defeat them alone.  We need allies. Powerful allies. I know it's a risk, but I know I have to take it.

quotes.sentence: Then send an emmissary! Don't go yourself!
quotes.originalSentence: Then send an emmissary! Don't go yourself!

quotes.sentence: Daenerys is a Queen. Only a King can convince her to help us. It has to be me.
quotes.originalSentence: Daenerys is a Queen. Only a King can convince her to help us. It has to be me.

quotes.sentence: You're abandoning your people! You're abandoning your home.
quotes.originalSentence: You're abandoning your people!  You're abandoning your home.

quotes.sentence: I'm leaving both in good hands.
quotes.originalSentence: I'm leaving both in good hands.

quotes.sentence: Whose?!
quotes.originalSentence: Whose?!

quotes.sentence: Yours. Until I return, the North is yours.
quotes.originalSentence: Yours. You're my sister, you're the only Stark in Winterfell. Until I return, the North is yours.

quotes.sentence: And Sansa? I hear she's alive and well.
quotes.originalSentence: And Sansa? I hear she's alive and well.

quotes.sentence: She is.
quotes.originalSentence: She is.

quotes.sentence: Does she miss me, terribly? A sham marriage... and unconsummated.
quotes.originalSentence: Does she miss me, terribly?  A sham marriage... and unconsummated.

quotes.sentence: I didn't ask.
quotes.originalSentence: I didn't ask.

quotes.sentence: She's much smarter than she lets on.
quotes.originalSentence: Well, it was. Wasn't. Anyway, she's much smarter than she lets on.

quotes.sentence: She's starting to let on.
quotes.originalSentence: She's starting to let on.

quotes.sentence: At some point, I want to hear how a Night's Watch recruit became King in the North.
quotes.originalSentence: Good. At some point, I want to hear how a Night's Watch recruit became King in the North.

quotes.sentence: As long as you tell me how a Lannister became Hand to Daenerys Targaryen.
quotes.originalSentence: As long as you tell me how a Lannister became Hand to Daenerys Targaryen.

quotes.sentence: A long and bloody tale. To be honest, I was drunk for most of it.
quotes.originalSentence: A long and bloody tale. To be honest, I was drunk for most of it.

quotes.sentence: My bannermen think I'm a fool for coming here.
quotes.originalSentence: My bannermen think I'm a fool for coming here.

quotes.sentence: Stark men don't fare well when they travel South.
quotes.originalSentence: Of course they do. If I was your Hand, I would've advised against it. General rule of thumb: Stark men don't fare well when they travel South.

quotes.sentence: True... but I'm not a Stark.
quotes.originalSentence: True... but I'm not a Stark.

quotes.sentence: I'd say you get used to them... but, you never really do. Their mother is waiting for you.
quotes.originalSentence: I'd say you get used to them... but, you never really do. Come. Their mother is waiting for you.

quotes.sentence: This is Jon Snow. He is King in the North.
quotes.originalSentence: ...This is Jon Snow. He is King in the North.

quotes.sentence: Thank you for travelling so far, my lord. I hope the seas were not too rough.
quotes.originalSentence: Thank you for travelling so far, my lord. I hope the seas were not too rough.

quotes.sentence: The winds were kind, your Grace.
quotes.originalSentence: The winds were kind, your Grace.

quotes.sentence: Jon Snow is King in the North, your Grace. He's not a lord.
quotes.originalSentence: Apologies, I have a Flea Bottom accent, I know, but Jon Snow is King in the North, your Grace. He's not a lord.

quotes.sentence: Forgive me...
quotes.originalSentence: Forgive me...

quotes.sentence: Your Grace, this is Ser Davos Seaworth.
quotes.originalSentence: Your Grace, this is Ser Davos Seaworth.

quotes.sentence: Forgive me, Ser Davos, I could have sworn I read the last King in the North was Torrhen Stark, who bent the knee to my ancestor.
quotes.originalSentence: Forgive me, Ser Davos, I never did receive a formal education, but I could have sworn I read the last King in the North was Torrhen Stark, who bent the knee to my ancestor, Aegon Targaryen. In exchange for his life and the lives of the Northmen, Torrhen Stark swore fealty to House Targaryen in perpetuity. Or do I have my facts wrong?

quotes.sentence: I wasn't there, your Grace.
quotes.originalSentence: I wasn't there, your Grace.

quotes.sentence: An oath is an oath, and perpetuity means... what does perpetuity mean, Lord Tyrion?
quotes.originalSentence: No, of course not, but still an oath is an oath, and perpetuity means... what does perpetuity mean, Lord Tyrion?

quotes.sentence: Forever.
quotes.originalSentence: Forever.

quotes.sentence: Forever. So I assume, my lord, you're here to bend the knee.
quotes.originalSentence: Forever. So I assume, my lord, you're here to bend the knee.

quotes.sentence: I am not.
quotes.originalSentence: I am not.

quotes.sentence: Oh? Well that is unfortunate. You've traveled all this way to break faith with House Targaryen?
quotes.originalSentence: Oh? Well that is unfortunate. You've traveled all this way to break faith with House Targaryen?

quotes.sentence: Break faith? Your father burnt my grandfather alive. He burnt my uncle alive.
quotes.originalSentence: Break faith? Your father burnt my grandfather alive. He burnt my uncle alive. He would have burned the Seven Kingdoms-

quotes.sentence: My father...was an evil man. I ask you not to judge a daughter by the sins of her father. Bend the knee and I will name you Warden of the North.
quotes.originalSentence: My father...was an evil man. On behalf of House Targaryen, I ask your forgiveness for the crimes he committed against your family, and I ask you not to judge a daughter by the sins of her father. Our two Houses were allies for centuries, and those were the best centuries the Seven Kingdoms have ever known. Centuries of peace and prosperity, with a Targaryen sitting on the Iron Throne and a Stark serving as Warden of the North. I am the last Targaryen, Jon Snow. Honor the pledge your ancestor made to mine. Bend the knee and I will name you Warden of the North. Together, we will save this country from those who would destroy it.

quotes.sentence: You're right. You're not guilty of your father's crimes, and I am not beholden to my ancestor's vows.
quotes.originalSentence: You're right. You're not guilty of your father's crimes, and I am not beholden to my ancestor's vows.

quotes.sentence: Then why are you here?
quotes.originalSentence: Then why are you here?

quotes.sentence: Because I need your help, and you need mine.
quotes.originalSentence: Because I need your help, and you need mine.

quotes.sentence: Did you see three dragons flying overhead when you arrived?
quotes.originalSentence: Did you see three dragons flying overhead when you arrived?

quotes.sentence: And did you see the Dothraki, all of whom have sworn to kill for me?
quotes.originalSentence: And did you see the Dothraki, all of whom have sworn to kill for me?

quotes.sentence: They're hard to miss.
quotes.originalSentence: They're hard to miss.

quotes.sentence: But still, I need your help?
quotes.originalSentence: But still, I need your help?

quotes.sentence: Not to defeat Cersei. You could storm King's Landing tomorrow and the city would fall.
quotes.originalSentence: Not to defeat Cersei. You could storm King's Landing tomorrow and the city would fall. Hell, we almost took it and we didn't even have dragons!

quotes.sentence: Almost.
quotes.originalSentence: Almost.

quotes.sentence: You don't want to kill thousands of innocent people...which means, at the very least, you're better than Cersei.
quotes.originalSentence: But you haven't stormed King's Landing. Why not? The only reason I can see is you don't want to kill thousands of innocent people. It's the fastest way to win the war, but you won't do it...which means, at the very least, you're better than Cersei.

quotes.sentence: Still, that doesn't explain why I need your help.
quotes.originalSentence: Still, that doesn't explain why I need your help.

quotes.sentence: We're children playing at a game, screaming that the rules aren't fair.
quotes.originalSentence: Because right now, you and I and Cersei and everyone else, we're children playing at a game, screaming that the rules aren't fair.

quotes.sentence: You told me you liked this man.
quotes.originalSentence: You told me you liked this man.

quotes.sentence: He's refused to call me Queen, he's refused to bow and now he's calling me a child!
quotes.originalSentence: In the time since he's met me, he's refused to call me Queen, he's refused to bow and now he's calling me a child!

quotes.sentence: I believe he's calling all of us children. Figure of speech.
quotes.originalSentence: I believe he's calling all of us children. Figure of speech.

quotes.sentence: Everyone you know will die before winter is over if we don't defeat the enemy to the north!
quotes.originalSentence: Your Grace, everyone you know will die before winter is over if we don't defeat the enemy to the north!

quotes.sentence: As far as I can see, you are the enemy to the north!
quotes.originalSentence: As far as I can see, you are the enemy to the north!

quotes.sentence: I am not your enemy! The dead are the enemy.
quotes.originalSentence: I am not your enemy! The dead are the enemy.

quotes.sentence: The dead? Is that another figure of speech?
quotes.originalSentence: The dead? Is that another figure of speech?

quotes.sentence: The army of the dead is on the march.
quotes.originalSentence: The army of the dead is on the march.

quotes.sentence: The army of the dead?
quotes.originalSentence: The army of the dead?

quotes.sentence: Do you think I'm a liar, or a madman?
quotes.originalSentence: You don't know me well, my lord, but do you think I'm a liar, or a madman?

quotes.sentence: No, I don't think you're either of those things.
quotes.originalSentence: No, I don't think you're either of those things.

quotes.sentence: The army of the dead is real. The White Walkers are real. The Night King is real. We're finished.
quotes.originalSentence: The army of the dead is real. The White Walkers are real. The Night King is real. I've seen them. If they get past the Wall and we're squabbling amongst ourselves, we're finished.

quotes.sentence: You'll be ruling over a graveyard if we don't defeat the Night King.
quotes.originalSentence: You'll be ruling over a graveyard if we don't defeat the Night King.

quotes.sentence: The war against my sister has already begun. You can't expect us to halt hostilities and join you in fighting...whatever you saw beyond the Wall.
quotes.originalSentence: The war against my sister has already begun. You can't expect us to halt hostilities and join you in fighting...whatever you saw beyond the Wall.

quotes.sentence: You don't believe him...He was named King in the North. Not because of his birthright. He's a damn bastard. All those hard sons of bitches chose him as their leader...because they believe in him. He took a knife in the heart for his people.
quotes.originalSentence: You don't believe him. I understand that. It sounds like nonsense. But if destiny has brought Daenerys Targaryen back to our shores, it has also made Jon Snow King in the North. You were the first to bring Dothraki to Westeros? He is the first to make allies with the wildlings and Northmen. He was named Lord Commander of the Night's Watch. He was named King in the North. Not because of his birthright. He has no birthright. He's a damn bastard. All those hard sons of bitches chose him as their leader...because they believe in him. All those things you don't believe in, he faced those things. He fought those things for the good of his people. He risked his life for his people. He took a knife in the heart for his people. He gave his own--

quotes.sentence: If we don't put aside our enmities and band together, we will die. And then it doesn't matter whose skeleton sits on the Iron Throne.
quotes.originalSentence: If we don't put aside our enmities and band together, we will die. And then it doesn't matter whose skeleton sits on the Iron Throne.

quotes.sentence: If it doesn't matter, then you might as well kneel. Swear your allegiance to Queen Daenerys.
quotes.originalSentence: If it doesn't matter, then you might as well kneel. Swear your allegiance to Queen Daenerys. Help her to defeat my sister, and together, our armies will protect the North.

quotes.sentence: There's no time for that. There's no time for any of this! While we stand here, debating--
quotes.originalSentence: There's no time for that. There's no time for any of this! While we stand here, debating--

quotes.sentence: It takes no time to bend the knee. Pledge your sword to her cause.
quotes.originalSentence: It takes no time to bend the knee. Pledge your sword to her cause.

quotes.sentence: And why would I do that? I don't know you. Your claim to the throne rests entirely on your father's name. The lords of the North placed their trust in me to lead them.
quotes.originalSentence: And why would I do that?  I mean no offense, Your Grace, but I don't know you. As far as I can tell, your claim to the throne rests entirely on your father's name, and my own father fought to overthrow the Mad King. The lords of the North placed their trust in me to lead them, and I will continue to do so as well as I can.

quotes.sentence: I was there that day when Ser Gregor crushed your lover's head. I can hear the sound of Oberyn's skull breaking, the sound of your scream... Myrcella did. She was mine and you took her from me! Why did you do that?!
quotes.originalSentence: I want you to know I understand. Even though we're enemies, you and I, I understand the fury that drives you. I was there that day when Ser Gregor crushed your lover's head. I close my eyes, I can hear the sound of Oberyn's skull breaking, the sound of your scream. I never heard a sound like that; I thought "That's true love". Oberyn looked beautiful that day, he really did. No one moved like him, no one had such skill with the spear, even Ser Gregor couldn't stop him. If only he hadn't taunted him: he could have walked away and left poor Ser Gregor to die. But that wasn't your lover's way, was it? Now he's buried somewhere, and here's Ser Gregor, stronger than ever. That must be difficult for you. When my daughter was taken from me, my only daughter...well, you can't imagine how that feels unless you've lost a child. I fed her at my own breast even though they told me to give her to the wet nurse; I couldn't bear to see her in another woman's arms. I never got to have a mother, but Myrcella did.  She was mine and you took her from me! Why did you do that?!

quotes.sentence: Your daughter's a beauty too... I imagine she's your favourite... You chose to murder my daughter; do you feel powerful now? I imagine ways of killing my enemies... I thought about having him crush your daughter's skull. The thought of this lovely face cracking open like a duck egg...no it's just not right!
quotes.originalSentence: Doesn't matter now. Your daughter's a beauty too. Those brown eyes, those lips, perfect Dornish beauty. I imagine she's your favourite. I know, I know we're not supposed to have favourites, but still, we're only human. We love whom we love.  I'm sorry, I can't understand you. The gag makes it impossible to understand what you're saying. Must be frustrating. We all make our choices. You chose to murder my daughter; you must have felt powerful when you made that choice. Do you feel powerful now? I don't sleep very well, not at all really. I lie in bed and I stare at the canopy and imagine ways of killing my enemies. How to destroy Ellaria Sand, the woman who murdered my only daughter?! I thought about having Ser Gregor crush your skull the way he did Oberyn's. It would be poetic, I suppose, but fast, too fast.  I thought about having him crush your daughter's skull. She's so beautiful.  The thought of this lovely face cracking open like a duck egg...no it's just not right!

quotes.sentence: Qyburn here is the cleverest man I know... Clever enough to learn what poison you used to murder Myrcella. The Long Goodbye, was that it?
quotes.originalSentence: Qyburn here is the cleverest man I know. Clever enough to learn what poison you used to murder Myrcella. The Long Goodbye, was that it?

quotes.sentence: The Long Farewell.
quotes.originalSentence: The Long Farewell.

quotes.sentence: That's the one. How long does the poison take?
quotes.originalSentence: That's the one. How long does the poison take?

quotes.sentence: Difficult to say. Hours, days, it depends on the subject's constitution.
quotes.originalSentence: Difficult to say. Hours, days, it depends on the subject's constitution.

quotes.sentence: But death is certain?
quotes.originalSentence: But death is certain?

quotes.sentence: Oh yes, Your Grace. Quite certain.
quotes.originalSentence: Oh yes, Your Grace. Quite certain.

quotes.sentence: You will live to watch your daughter rot.
quotes.originalSentence: Your daughter will die here, in this cell and you'll be here watching when she does. You'll be here the rest of your days. If you refuse to eat, we'll force food down your throat. You will live to watch your daughter rot, to watch that beautiful face collapse to bone and dust, all the while contemplating the choices you've made.  Make sure the guards change the torches every few hours. I don't want her to miss a thing.

quotes.sentence: It's done?
quotes.originalSentence: It's done?

quotes.sentence: It is.
quotes.originalSentence: It is.

quotes.sentence: "And now the rains weep o'er our halls."
quotes.originalSentence: "And now the rains weep o'er our halls." Did we fight well?

quotes.sentence: As well as could be expected.
quotes.originalSentence: As well as could be expected.

quotes.sentence: Golden roses, indeed!
quotes.originalSentence: It was never really our forte. Golden roses, indeed! Your brother and his new queen thought you would be defending Casterly Rock.

quotes.sentence: Casterly Rock isn't worth much any more.

quotes.originalSentence: You don't know me well, Your Grace, but I know Ser Jaime. He is a man of honor. I was his captor once, but when we were both taken prisoner and the men holding us tried to force themselves on me, Ser Jaime defended me. And lost his hand because of it.  Without him, my lady, you would not be alive. He armed me, armoured me and sent me to find you and bring you home, because he'd sworn an oath to your mother.

And yet, I can't help but feel... we're at odds with one another. Why is that? Your brother?

Until I met Jon.  Now I'm here, half a world away, fighting Jon's war, alongside him.  Tell me, who manipulated whom?

Our fellow men and women, who set aside their differences, to fight together... and die together, so that others might live.  Everyone in this world, owes them a debt that can never be repaid.  It is our duty and our honor, to keep them alive in memory, for those who come after us, and those who come after them... for as long as men draw breath.  They were the shields that guarded the realms of men.  And we shall never see their like again.
